.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:283:- Fixture success proves detector integrity for the covered regex or heuristic lanes only; it does not prove user-visible structural quality, routing quality, or graph-context usefulness.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:300:Packet `011-graph-payload-validator-and-trust-preservation` hardens emission boundaries that surface structural trust. The implementation remains additive to packet `006`: import `ParserProvenance`, `EvidenceStatus`, `FreshnessAuthority`, and the shared validation helpers from `lib/context/shared-payload.ts` instead of defining a graph-local contract.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:304:- Emit code-graph or bridge-facing payloads only after validating that `parserProvenance`, `evidenceStatus`, and `freshnessAuthority` are all present as separate fields.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:305:- Fail closed when any axis is missing, malformed, or collapsed into scalar stand-ins such as `trust`, `trustScore`, `confidence`, `confidenceScore`, or `authorityScore`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:306:- Preserve the three axes distinctly through bootstrap or resume-facing payloads. Do not merge them into a single "graph trust" label during transport.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:310:- Packet `011` does not introduce a parallel graph-only trust contract family.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:311:- Current owner surfaces remain authoritative: shared payload sections, session bootstrap or resume consumers, and bridge-facing graph payloads.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/contracts/README.md:312:- Ranking confidence and other retrieval-ordering metadata stay separate from `StructuralTrust`.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:5:Complete installation and configuration guide for the Spec Kit Memory MCP server. This guide enables AI-powered context retrieval and conversation memory across your project. The system indexes markdown documentation from spec folders and constitutional rules to surface relevant information during AI interactions. It provides 43 tools covering semantic search, trigger-based memory surfacing, intent-aware context loading, causal relationship tracking, shared memory spaces, session learning, evaluation, validation, and bounded structural code-graph indexing.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:83:│    mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite   (structural graph)   │
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:90:│ Docs         │ │ (memory lineage) │ │ code-graph.sqlite          │
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:94:│ Spec docs    │ │ reranking       │ │ web-tree-sitter (WASM)     │
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:114:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite` | Default repo-local structural code-graph database used by the checked-in configs |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:120:- `code-graph.sqlite` (auto-created on first `code_graph_scan`, stored alongside `context-index.sqlite`)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:124:Current code-graph behavior is intentionally bounded:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:125:- startup and resume surfaces report freshness-aware graph status (`fresh`, `stale`, `empty`, `error`)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:126:- `code_graph_query` and `code_graph_context` may repair small stale deltas inline
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:127:- empty or broadly stale graphs still require explicit `code_graph_scan`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:137:| Codex | `.codex/config.toml` | Checked-in MCP config. Bootstrap parity via `session_bootstrap` MCP tool, not a native SessionStart hook. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:355:| `SPECKIT_MEMORY_GRAPH_UNIFIED` | `true` | Default-on memory-roadmap graph capability metadata. Distinct from the live `SPECKIT_GRAPH_UNIFIED` runtime retrieval gate. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:373:        "SPECKIT_MEMORY_ROADMAP_PHASE": "graph",
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:426:- `memory_context` (unified context retrieval)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:427:- `memory_search` (semantic search)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:428:- `memory_match_triggers` (fast trigger matching)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:432:- `code_graph_scan` (structural code indexing)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:433:- `code_graph_query` (structural relationship queries)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:434:- `code_graph_status` (graph health check)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:435:- `code_graph_context` (LLM-oriented graph neighborhoods)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:437:- `session_bootstrap` (complete session bootstrap)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:438:- `session_resume` (combined session resume)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:443:Run session bootstrap, then query the code graph for symbols in .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:447:- the startup or bootstrap response reports freshness-aware graph status
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:449:- if the graph is only lightly stale, the read path may refresh inline before returning results
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:465:- [ ] `memory_search()` returns results (or empty if no memories are indexed yet)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:530:### memory_context: Unified Context Retrieval
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:532:`memory_context()` is the primary entry point for context loading. It detects task intent and routes to the optimal retrieval strategy automatically.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:548:### memory_search: Semantic Search
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:550:`memory_search()` runs vector-based similarity search across all indexed memories.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:559:### memory_match_triggers: Fast Keyword Lookup
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:561:`memory_match_triggers()` provides sub-50ms keyword-based matching. Use it for immediate context surfacing at the start of a conversation.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:600:Cross-encoder reranking is enabled by default. The reranker applies a second-pass scoring pass over the top-K candidates returned by the initial retrieval, improving result ordering for ambiguous queries.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:604:When Z-score analysis signals low-confidence retrieval (insufficient signal in the indexed corpus), the server prepends an evidence gap warning to the LLM payload. This tells the AI assistant that results may be incomplete rather than letting it treat sparse results as authoritative.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:614:- `quality`: Result quality score based on embedding confidence and match density
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:620:Routes retrieval requests through per-type strategies based on the artifact being queried. Supports 8 artifact types: `spec`, `plan`, `checklist`, `decision-record`, `memory`, `implementation-summary`, `research` and `handover`. Each type applies its own indexing and ranking rules (for example, `memory` uses recency-weighted scoring while `decision-record` boosts causal edges).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:629:- `ContextEnvelope`: Wraps all `memory_context()` responses with metadata (mode used, memories returned, intent detected)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:681:**Behind the scenes:** The AI calls `memory_context()` with `mode: "resume"` and `anchors: ["state", "next-steps"]`, then returns the previous session state.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:692:1. AI calls `memory_context({ input: "add user profiles", mode: "auto" })`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:791:| `code_graph_query` reports `full_scan` or `inline full scan skipped for read path` | The graph is empty or too stale for bounded read-path repair | Run `code_graph_scan`, then retry the structural read |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:792:| Startup or resume shows graph `stale` | Freshness-aware startup detected drift before a structural read ran | Run a structural read to allow bounded inline repair, or run `code_graph_scan` for broader stale states |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:982:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite` | Structural code-graph database |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:1047:MCP TOOLS: memory_context, memory_search, memory_match_triggers,
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:1064:| v1.7.x | 2026-02-20 | Cross-encoder reranking enabled by default. Co-activation score boost fix. Query expansion on deep mode. Evidence gap warnings. MMR reranking with intent-mapped lambda. Phase system support (recursive validation, phase detection scoring). Feature flag updates. `memory_context` tokenUsage parameter. 28-tool surface area. |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:134:  - `README.md` — relocation note and local compatibility documentation for retrieval-trace contracts.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:135:  - `../../shared/contracts/retrieval-trace.ts` — canonical shared contract implementation referenced by retrieval and telemetry code.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:204:### `graph/`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:206:- Purpose: Owns graph-derived retrieval signals and community detection over memory relationships. It is the canonical place for graph algorithms that operate over causal/community structure rather than primary retrieval channels.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:209:  - `graph-signals.ts` — momentum and causal-depth signal calculation.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:210:  - `README.md` — graph-feature overview and runtime flags.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:257:  - `memory-parser.ts` — main parser for anchors, metadata, causal links, and document classification.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:297:- Purpose: Owns ranking and calibration logic once candidate memories already exist. It combines importance tiers, composite scoring, folder relevance, confidence signals, and negative-feedback effects.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:302:  - `confidence-tracker.ts` — confidence-related normalization helpers.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:303:  - `negative-feedback.ts` — post-feedback confidence penalties.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:313:- Purpose: Owns the retrieval engine: candidate generation, vector/lexical channels, fusion, reranking, query intelligence, graph-aware retrieval, and schema/index helpers needed by the search path. This is the highest-churn and broadest domain module in `lib/`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:354:  - `causal-edges.ts` — causal edge persistence and graph-maintenance hooks.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:366:- Purpose: Owns structured observability for retrieval and scoring behavior: trace sanitization, retrieval telemetry, scoring observability, and consumption logging. It is intended to observe domain modules rather than drive domain decisions.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:370:  - `trace-schema.ts` — trace payload sanitization and schema validation.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:424:- `10--graph-signal-activation`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:451:| `graph` | `06--analysis`, `10--graph-signal-activation`, `15--retrieval-enhancements` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:454:| `manage` | `04--maintenance`, `10--graph-signal-activation`, `11--scoring-and-calibration` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:459:| `scoring` | `10--graph-signal-activation`, `11--scoring-and-calibration`, `15--retrieval-enhancements` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:460:| `search` | `01--retrieval`, `03--discovery`, `06--analysis`, `10--graph-signal-activation`, `11--scoring-and-calibration`, `12--query-intelligence`, `14--pipeline-architecture`, `15--retrieval-enhancements`, `19--feature-flag-reference` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:463:| `storage` | `02--mutation`, `04--maintenance`, `05--lifecycle`, `08--bug-fixes-and-data-integrity`, `10--graph-signal-activation`, `13--memory-quality-and-indexing`, `17--governance` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:477:| `06--analysis` | `graph`, `search` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:481:| `10--graph-signal-activation` | `graph`, `manage`, `scoring`, `search`, `storage` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:486:| `15--retrieval-enhancements` | `cache`, `chunking`, `cognitive`, `graph`, `providers`, `scoring`, `search`, `session` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:554:- `telemetry` should accept trace/metric payloads from `search`, `scoring`, or handlers, but not import retrieval logic from them.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:585:- `graph`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:609:- `graph`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:613:  - May import: core, `cognitive`, `graph`, `config`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:617:  - This is the top domain aggregator and is allowed to consume `cache`, `providers`, `session`, `storage`, `parsing`, `cognitive`, `graph`, `scoring`, `governance`, and `collab`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/MODULE_MAP.md:623:  - May import: core, `storage`, `graph`, `scoring`

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:6:  - "graph signals"

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:14:> Community detection and graph signal scoring for causal memory networks. Provides BFS connected-component labelling, single-level Louvain modularity and momentum/depth scoring.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:33:The graph module operates on the `causal_edges` and `memory_index` tables to detect communities, compute scoring signals, manage temporal edge validity, detect contradictions, track usage, and generate community summaries. All features are independently gated behind `SPECKIT_*` feature flags.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:40:- **Graph signals** compute two additive score bonuses for search results: momentum (degree change over 7 days) and causal depth (normalized longest-path depth on the SCC-condensed causal DAG).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:41:- **Temporal edges** add `valid_at`/`invalid_at` columns to `causal_edges` for bi-temporal validity management.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:50:| BFS-first, Louvain-second | BFS runs in O(V+E) and handles well-partitioned graphs. Louvain is only invoked when BFS produces a single dominant component. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:64:graph/
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:69:  graph-signals.ts            # Momentum and causal depth scoring, degree snapshots
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:84:| `graph-signals.ts` | Degree snapshots, momentum scoring (7-day delta), causal depth via SCC condensation | `SPECKIT_GRAPH_SIGNALS` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:85:| `temporal-edges.ts` | Adds `valid_at`/`invalid_at` columns to `causal_edges`, provides `invalidateEdge()` and `getValidEdges()` | `SPECKIT_TEMPORAL_EDGES` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:101:| `snapshotDegrees` | graph-signals.ts | Records current degree counts into `degree_snapshots` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:102:| `computeMomentum` | graph-signals.ts | Computes single-node momentum (degree delta over 7 days) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:103:| `computeMomentumScores` | graph-signals.ts | Batch momentum computation with session caching |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:104:| `computeCausalDepthScores` | graph-signals.ts | Batch normalized causal depth via SCC condensation and longest-path DAG traversal |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:105:| `applyGraphSignals` | graph-signals.ts | Applies momentum (+0.05 max) and depth (+0.05 max) bonuses to result rows |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:106:| `clearGraphSignalsCache` | graph-signals.ts | Clears momentum and depth session caches |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:110:| `ensureTemporalColumns` | temporal-edges.ts | Adds valid_at/invalid_at columns to causal_edges |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:138:| `causal_edges` | Multiple | Source of graph structure; temporal-edges adds valid_at/invalid_at |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/graph/README.md:141:| `degree_snapshots` | graph-signals.ts | Historical degree counts per snapshot date |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/manage/README.md:12:> Reserved for future batch-management utilities. The former graph-authority helper was removed after the dead-code audit confirmed it was never wired into the live system.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/manage/README.md:34:The former graph-authority helper was removed because the active retrieval pipeline uses typed-weighted degree scoring and graph signal helpers instead.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/manage/README.md:78:When graph scoring behavior needs review, start with the active modules listed below instead of looking for helpers in `lib/manage/`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/manage/README.md:87:- `../search/graph-search-fn.ts` — typed-weighted degree scoring used by the live retrieval path.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/manage/README.md:88:- `../graph/graph-signals.ts` — graph signal aggregation used by active scoring.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/extraction/README.md:119:| **Edge Density Check** | `computeEdgeDensity()` returns the ratio of causal edges to total memories |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/architecture/README.md:32:The architecture module defines the 7-layer MCP tool organization (T060) that enables progressive disclosure from high-level orchestration to specialized operations. .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:43:  description: '[L1:Orchestration] Unified entry point for context retrieval with intent-aware routing. START HERE for most memory operations. For session recovery, use mode: \'resume\' with profile: \'resume\'. Automatically detects task intent (add_feature, fix_bug, refactor, security_audit, understand, find_spec, find_decision) and routes to optimal retrieval strategy. Modes: auto (default), quick (trigger-based), deep (comprehensive), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery). Token Budget: 3500. For code search by concept/intent, prefer mcp__cocoindex_code__search (CocoIndex). For structural code queries (callers, imports), prefer code_graph_query.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:44:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { input: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'The query, prompt, or context description (required)' }, mode: { type: 'string', enum: ['auto', 'quick', 'deep', 'focused', 'resume'], default: 'auto', description: 'Context retrieval mode: auto (detect intent), quick (fast triggers), deep (comprehensive search), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery)' }, intent: { type: 'string', enum: ['add_feature', 'fix_bug', 'refactor', 'security_audit', 'understand', 'find_spec', 'find_decision'], description: 'Explicit task intent. If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. When provided, it must match an existing server-managed session or the call is rejected. Omit it to let the server generate a new session for this request.' }, enableDedup: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable session deduplication' }, includeContent: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include full file content in results' }, includeTrace: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include provenance-rich trace data (scores, source, trace) in results when underlying memory_search is called' }, tokenUsage: { type: 'number', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 1.0, description: "Optional caller token usage ratio (0.0-1.0)" }, anchors: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Filter content to specific anchors (e.g., ["state", "next-steps"] for resume mode)' }, profile: { type: 'string', enum: ['quick', 'research', 'resume', 'debug'], description: 'Optional response profile formatter. Returns a reduced or mode-aware response shape when profile formatting is enabled.' } }, required: ['input'] },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:49:  name: 'memory_search',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:50:  description: '[L2:Core] Search conversation memories semantically using vector similarity. Returns ranked results with similarity scores. Constitutional tier memories are ALWAYS included at the top of results (~2000 tokens max), regardless of query. Requires query (string), concepts (array of 2-5 strings), or cursor (string) for continuation pagination. Supports intent-aware retrieval (REQ-006) with task-specific weight adjustments. When implicit feedback logging is enabled, searches also emit shadow-only feedback signals such as search_shown and, for includeContent runs, result_cited. Token Budget: 3500. For code search by concept/intent, prefer mcp__cocoindex_code__search (CocoIndex). For structural code queries (callers, imports), prefer code_graph_query.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:54:    'x-requiredAnyOf': [['query'], ['concepts'], ['cursor']],
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:56:      cursor: {
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:59:        description: 'Opaque continuation cursor returned by progressive disclosure. When provided, resolves the next page without requiring a new query.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:99:        description: 'Enable causal-neighbor boost (2-hop traversal on causal_edges). Defaults to SPECKIT_CAUSAL_BOOST env flag.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:133:      rerank: {
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:136:        description: 'Enable cross-encoder reranking of results. Improves relevance at the cost of additional computation.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:141:        description: 'Apply length-based penalty during reranking. Penalizes very long memories to favor concise, focused results. Only effective when rerank is true.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:184:        description: 'When true (or when SPECKIT_RESPONSE_TRACE=true), include provenance-rich scores/source/trace envelope fields in each result.'
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:190:// E3: Simplified search — 3 params, sensible defaults, delegates to memory_search
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:193:  description: '[L2:Core] Simplified search — query + optional limit + optional spec folder. Delegates to memory_search with sensible defaults (intent auto-detect ON, dedup ON, content included, limit 10). Use this when you want fast search without configuring 31 parameters.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:211:  name: 'memory_match_triggers',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:299:  description: '[L4:Mutation] Record validation feedback for a memory. Tracks whether memories are useful, updating confidence scores. Memories with high confidence and validation counts may be promoted to critical tier. Token Budget: 500.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:305:      wasUseful: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Whether the memory was useful (true increases confidence, false decreases it)' },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:485:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Trace causal chain for a memory to answer "why was this decision made?" Traverses causal edges up to maxDepth hops, grouping results by relationship type (caused, enabled, supersedes, contradicts, derived_from, supports). Use to understand decision lineage and memory relationships. Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:487:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { memoryId: { type: 'string', description: 'Memory ID to trace causal lineage for (number or string, required)' }, maxDepth: { type: 'number', default: 3, minimum: 1, maximum: 10, description: 'Maximum traversal depth (default: 3, max: 10)' }, direction: { type: 'string', description: 'Traversal direction: outgoing, incoming, or both (default: both)' }, relations: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Filter to specific relationship types: caused, enabled, supersedes, contradicts, derived_from, supports' }, includeMemoryDetails: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Include full memory details in results' } }, required: ['memoryId'] },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:491:  name: 'memory_causal_link',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:492:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Create a causal relationship between two memories. Links represent decision lineage (caused, enabled), versioning (supersedes), contradictions, derivation, or support. Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:497:  name: 'memory_causal_stats',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:498:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Get statistics about the causal memory graph. Shows total edges, coverage percentage, and breakdown by relationship type. Target: 60% of memories linked (CHK-065). Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:503:  name: 'memory_causal_unlink',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:504:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Remove a causal relationship by edge ID. Use memory_drift_why to find edge IDs. Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:522:        items: { type: 'string', enum: ['vector', 'bm25', 'fts5', 'graph', 'trigger'] },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:624:  name: 'code_graph_scan',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:625:  description: '[L7:Maintenance] Scan workspace files and build structural code graph index (functions, classes, imports, calls). Supports incremental re-indexing via content hash. Token Budget: 1000.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:639:  name: 'code_graph_query',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:640:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Query structural relationships: outline (file symbols), calls_from/calls_to (call graph), imports_from/imports_to (dependency graph), and blast_radius (reverse import impact). Use INSTEAD of Grep for structural queries (callers, imports, dependencies). Supports includeTransitive for multi-hop BFS traversal. Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:657:  name: 'code_graph_status',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:658:  description: '[L7:Maintenance] Report code graph index health: file count, node/edge counts by type, parse health summary, last scan timestamp, DB file size, schema version. Token Budget: 500.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:663:  name: 'code_graph_context',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:664:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Get LLM-oriented compact graph neighborhoods. Accepts CocoIndex search results as seeds — use CocoIndex (mcp__cocoindex_code__search) for semantic search first, then pass results here for structural expansion. Supports manual seeds (provider: manual) and graph seeds (provider: graph). Modes: neighborhood (1-hop calls+imports), outline (file symbols), impact (reverse callers). Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:680:            provider: { type: 'string', enum: ['cocoindex', 'manual', 'graph'], description: 'Seed provider type' },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:681:            file: { type: 'string', description: 'CocoIndex file path (provider: cocoindex)' },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:688:        description: 'Seeds from CocoIndex, manual input, or graph lookups',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:692:      includeTrace: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Include trace metadata in response for debugging' },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:734:  description: '[L3:Discovery] Check session readiness: priming status, code graph freshness, time since last tool call. Call periodically during long sessions to check for context drift. Returns ok/warning/stale with actionable hints. No arguments required.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:740:  name: 'session_resume',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:741:  description: '[L1:Orchestration] Resume session with combined memory, code graph, and CocoIndex status in a single call. Use when you want the detailed merged resume payload directly. For the canonical first-call recovery path on session start or after /clear, prefer session_bootstrap. Use minimal: true to skip the heavy memory context call and return code graph, CocoIndex, structural context, hints, and session-quality metadata without the full memory payload.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:747:      minimal: { type: 'boolean', description: 'When true, skip the heavy memory context call and return code-graph, CocoIndex, structural-context, hints, and session-quality fields without the full memory payload' },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:755:  name: 'session_bootstrap',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:756:  description: '[L1:Orchestration] Complete session bootstrap in one call. Returns session context, system health, structural readiness, and recommended next actions. This is the canonical first recovery call on session start or after /clear; it wraps the full session_resume payload plus session_health.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:768:      resume: { type: 'object', description: 'Merged session_resume payload (spec folder, task status, memory context)' },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:770:      structuralContext: { type: 'object', description: 'Structural bootstrap contract (status, summary, recommendedAction); omitted when code graph is unavailable', properties: { status: { type: 'string', enum: ['ready', 'stale', 'missing'] }, summary: { type: 'string' }, recommendedAction: { type: 'string' } } },
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:780:  name: 'deep_loop_graph_upsert',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:781:  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Idempotent upsert for coverage graph nodes and edges. Reducer writes graph deltas after each deep-loop iteration. Rejects self-loops, clamps weights to [0.0, 2.0], and merges metadata updates on repeated IDs. Requires specFolder, loopType, and sessionId for namespace isolation.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:826:  name: 'deep_loop_graph_query',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:827:  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Structured analysis of deep-loop coverage graph state. Supports query types: uncovered_questions (questions with no coverage), unverified_claims (claims without verification), contradictions (CONTRADICTS edge pairs), provenance_chain (BFS from a node following citation/evidence edges), coverage_gaps (nodes missing incoming coverage edges), and hot_nodes (most connected nodes by edge count + weight).',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:844:  name: 'deep_loop_graph_status',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:845:  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Report deep-loop coverage graph health: node/edge counts grouped by kind and relation, current convergence signal values, momentum (signal deltas between snapshots), last iteration, schema version, and DB file size. Suitable for dashboards and synthesis surfaces.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:857:  name: 'deep_loop_graph_convergence',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:858:  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Composite convergence assessment for deep-loop coverage graph. Returns a typed decision (CONTINUE, STOP_ALLOWED, STOP_BLOCKED), signal values, blockers with severity levels, and a typed trace explaining each signal threshold evaluation. For research: evaluates questionCoverage, claimVerificationRate, contradictionDensity, plus blocking guards sourceDiversity and evidenceDepth. For review: evaluates dimensionCoverage, findingStability, p0ResolutionRate, evidenceDensity, hotspotSaturation. Extends Phase 001 stop logic without replacing newInfoRatio.',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:3:description: "5-channel hybrid search architecture combining vector, lexical (BM25/FTS5), graph-based and structure-aware graph retrieval with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and Adaptive Fusion."
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:12:> 5-channel hybrid search architecture combining vector, lexical (BM25/FTS5), graph-based and structure-aware graph retrieval, fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and Adaptive Fusion.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:24:  - [GRAPH SIGNAL FEATURES](#graph-signal-features)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:39:The search subsystem provides production-grade hybrid search capabilities with multiple retrieval methods fused via RRF scoring. It handles query expansion, intent classification, typo tolerance and optional cross-encoder reranking.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:96:| Graph | `graph-search-fn.ts` | Causal edge traversal + typed-weighted degree (R4) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:97:| Degree | `graph-search-fn.ts` | Structural graph discovery via typed-weighted degree |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:105:2. Causal boost — graph-traversal neighbor amplification
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:107:4. Graph signals (N2a + N2b) — momentum + causal depth
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:115:**Stage 3 — Rerank + Aggregate** (`stage3-rerank.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:116:Cross-encoder reranking (optional, min 2 results) followed by MPAB chunk-to-memory aggregation. Aggregation formula: `parentScore = sMax + 0.3 * Sum(rest) / sqrt(N)` where `sMax` is the best chunk score and N is the remaining chunk count. Chunk ordering preserves `chunk_index` document order (B2 guarantee). `contentSource` metadata marks provenance (`reassembled_chunks` or `file_read_fallback`).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:119:**Score immutability invariant**: Stage 4 MUST NOT modify scores. Enforced via compile-time `Stage4ReadonlyRow` readonly fields and runtime `captureScoreSnapshot` / `verifyScoreInvariant` defence-in-depth. Applies memory-state filtering (HOT/WARM/COLD/DORMANT/ARCHIVED with per-tier limits), evidence gap detection (Z-score confidence check), quality floor (`QUALITY_FLOOR=0.005`), and token budget truncation.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:141:- **MMR Diversity**: Maximal Marginal Relevance reranking reduces near-duplicate results
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:143:- **Co-activation Boost**: Graph-neighbor score propagation via 2-hop causal traversal
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:175:Packet `026-graph-and-context-optimization/010-fts-capability-cascade-floor` freezes the lexical capability contract that packet `002-implement-cache-warning-hooks` now consumes. `memory_search` responses expose:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:220:- **Voyage rerank-2**: API-based, max 100 docs
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:221:- **Cohere rerank-english-v3.0**: API-based, max 100 docs
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:222:- **Local GGUF path**: `node-llama-cpp` with `bge-reranker-v2-m3.Q4_K_M.gguf` when `RERANKER_LOCAL=true` and the runtime/model guards pass
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:248:| **TypeScript**       | `reranker.ts` (local reranking utility); `rrf-fusion.ts`, `adaptive-fusion.ts`, `mmr-reranker.ts` relocated to `shared/algorithms/` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:250:| **TypeScript**       | `channel-representation.ts`, `channel-enforcement.ts`, `confidence-truncation.ts` (quality)   |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:290:| `hybrid-search.ts`         | ~900   | TypeScript | Orchestrates vector/FTS/BM25/graph/degree fusion via adaptive RRF |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:292:| `local-reranker.ts`        | -      | TypeScript | Local GGUF-based cross-encoder reranking fallback   |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:305:| `confidence-truncation.ts` | -      | TypeScript | Removes low-confidence tail using 2x median gap heuristic (min 3 results) (Sprint 3) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:309:| `evidence-gap-detector.ts` | -      | TypeScript | Z-score confidence check on RRF scores to detect low-confidence retrieval |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:310:| `fsrs.ts`                  | -      | TypeScript | Temporal-structural coherence: FSRS stability augmented with graph centrality |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:333:| `confidence-scoring.ts`    | -      | TypeScript | Computes calibrated confidence scores for retrieval results |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:335:| `graph-calibration.ts`     | -      | TypeScript | Calibrates graph-channel scoring inputs and weights |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:336:| `graph-lifecycle.ts`       | -      | TypeScript | Manages graph refresh, rebuild, and lifecycle operations |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:340:| `progressive-disclosure.ts` | -     | TypeScript | Supports progressive-disclosure result paging and response shaping |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:353:**Relocated to `shared/algorithms/`**: `rrf-fusion.ts`, `adaptive-fusion.ts`, `mmr-reranker.ts` -- these are now imported from `@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/`.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:414:| `SPECKIT_CROSS_ENCODER`  | `true`   | Enable cross-encoder reranking gate |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:523:- If no structural graph channel: Vector + Lexical + Co-activation (3-channel fallback)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:548:// 1. VOYAGE_API_KEY -> Voyage rerank-2
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:612:**Purpose**: Create causal edges between memories that share extracted entities across spec folders. Gated via `SPECKIT_ENTITY_LINKING`. Requires R10 (`SPECKIT_AUTO_ENTITIES`) to also be enabled.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:620:| **Edge Creation** | Creates `supports` causal edges between memories sharing entities |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:621:| **Density Guard** | Max 20 edges per node to prevent graph density explosion |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:631:| `createEntityLinks` | `(db, matches) => EntityLinkResult` | Create causal edges for cross-doc matches |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:636:<a id="graph-signal-features"></a>
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:641:**R4: Typed-Weighted Degree** (`graph-search-fn.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:646:| `caused` | 1.0 | Strongest causal signal |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:656:Top-ranked results seed a 2-hop BFS traversal over causal edges. Discovered neighbors receive a score boost with `SEED_FRACTION=0.25` strength. A `1/sqrt(neighbor_count)` fan-effect divisor (R17) prevents hub nodes from appearing in every co-activation result.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:658:**N2a: Graph Momentum** (`graph-signals.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:661:**N2b: Causal Depth** (`graph-signals.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:662:The causal graph is condensed into strongly connected components, then longest-path depth is computed across the resulting DAG. Shortcut edges do not collapse deeper chains; cycle members share one bounded depth layer, and the final depth is normalized by the deepest reachable component chain (`maxDepth`). Score bonus: `normalizedDepth * 0.05` — rewards structurally deep nodes in the causal chain.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:665:BFS connected-component labelling assigns community IDs. When the largest component contains >50% of all nodes, escalates to Louvain modularity optimization for finer-grained communities. Community co-members are injected into Stage 2 results before graph signal scoring. Gated via `SPECKIT_COMMUNITY_DETECTION`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:668:Measures graph density and reports metrics used for R10 entity extraction escalation decisions.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:698:| 0.75 - 0.88 | **Conflict** | Supersede: deprecate existing, create `supersedes` causal edge |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:736:**Degree Cache Invalidation** (`graph-search-fn.ts`):

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/mcp-error-format.vitest.ts:122:        name: 'Causal graph handler error format on invalid input',
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:3:description: "Multi-factor scoring system for memory retrieval with composite weighting, importance tiers, folder ranking and confidence tracking."
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:12:> Multi-factor scoring system for memory retrieval with composite weighting, importance tiers, folder ranking and confidence tracking.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:48:| **HVR Integration** | Human Validation Rate integration for confidence-weighted scoring (Spec 137) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:50:| **Graph Signals** | Optional post-score momentum and causal-depth bonuses applied after core scoring |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:125:HVR integration extends the confidence tracking system by incorporating human validation signals into composite scoring. The HVR score reflects how often users confirm a memory as useful versus not useful, creating a feedback-weighted confidence metric.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:132:- Works alongside the existing promotion pipeline in `confidence-tracker.ts`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:146:Graph signals are applied after the core scoring pipeline via `lib/graph/graph-signals.ts`. When enabled, they add two bounded bonuses to the current score:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:151:The causal-depth pass condenses strongly connected components, then computes longest-path depth across the resulting DAG. This preserves deeper causal chains even when shortcut edges exist, gives all members of a cycle the same bounded depth layer, and still leaves pure isolated cycles at `0` normalized depth.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:3:description: "Retrieval telemetry, scoring observability, trace schema validation, and consumption logging for the MCP server pipeline."
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:10:  - "trace schema"
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:15:> Retrieval telemetry, scoring observability, trace schema validation, and consumption logging for the MCP server pipeline.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:35:The telemetry module provides structured observability for the retrieval pipeline and scoring subsystem. It records per-stage latency, search mode selection, fallback triggers, composite quality scores, scoring observation samples, trace payload validation, and agent consumption events. Telemetry data flows to governance tooling and is used by retrieval handlers to surface pipeline health metrics.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:41:| Modules | 4 | `retrieval-telemetry.ts`, `scoring-observability.ts`, `trace-schema.ts`, `consumption-logger.ts` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:50:| **LatencyMetrics** | Stage-by-stage timing: candidate, fusion, rerank, boost, and total |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:54:| **TelemetryTracePayload** | Canonical retrieval trace payload (sanitized, no sensitive/extra fields) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:69:├── trace-schema.ts           # Canonical trace payload schema and validation
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:80:| `trace-schema.ts` | Canonical schema and runtime validation for `TelemetryTracePayload`; sanitizes unknown/sensitive fields from trace data |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:94:| `SPECKIT_EXTENDED_TELEMETRY` | `false` | Enable extended metric collection (latency breakdown, quality scoring, trace payload validation, and architecture updates). Set to `true` to activate |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:108:When `SPECKIT_EXTENDED_TELEMETRY` is disabled (default), the minimal `RetrievalTelemetry` shell is still created so callers can rely on a stable shape. Latency, mode, fallback, quality, graph-health, and adaptive sub-metrics remain zeroed/empty, while the baseline architecture snapshot still records the current memory-roadmap phase/capability defaults.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:123:| `graphHealth` | `GraphHealthMetrics` | Graph-signal injection and rollback-gate state for the run |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:125:| `transitionDiagnostics` | `SessionTransitionTrace \| undefined` | Optional spec-shaped session transition diagnostics captured for trace-enabled retrievals |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:126:| `graphWalkDiagnostics` | `GraphWalkDiagnostics \| undefined` | Optional graph-walk rollout and contribution diagnostics captured for retrieval observability |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:128:| `tracePayload` | `TelemetryTracePayload \| undefined` | Optional canonical retrieval trace payload |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:139:| `rerankLatencyMs` | `number` | Cross-encoder rerank duration (0 if skipped) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/README.md:140:| `boostLatencyMs` | `number` | Session/causal boost duration |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:3:description: "Four-stage retrieval pipeline: candidate generation, fusion, reranking and filtering."
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:10:  - "reranking"
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:18:A four-stage retrieval pipeline that takes a search query through candidate generation, score fusion, reranking and final filtering to produce ranked memory results.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:34:The `pipeline/` directory implements the core retrieval pipeline behind `memory_search`. Each search request flows through four sequential stages, each with a defined I/O contract and clear responsibility boundary. The pipeline supports hybrid, vector and multi-concept search types with optional deep-mode query expansion, cross-encoder reranking, MMR diversity pruning and MPAB chunk-to-parent reassembly.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:47:| `stage2-fusion.ts` | Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration. The single authoritative scoring point. Applies 9 signal steps in fixed order: session boost, causal boost, co-activation spreading, community co-retrieval, graph signals, FSRS testing effect, intent weights (non-hybrid only, G2 prevention), artifact routing, feedback signals, artifact limiting, anchor metadata and validation metadata scoring. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:48:| `stage3-rerank.ts` | Stage 3: Rerank + Aggregate. Applies cross-encoder reranking (remote or local GGUF), MMR diversity pruning and MPAB chunk collapse with parent document reassembly from the database. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:64:      |  session/causal/co-activation boosts, intent weights,
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:68:      |  cross-encoder reranking, MMR diversity, MPAB chunk collapse
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:73:  PipelineResult { results, metadata, annotations, trace }
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:84:- The only stage where scoring modifications happen (except Stage 3 reranking).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:89:**Stage 3 - Rerank + Aggregate** (`stage3-rerank.ts`)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:90:- Cross-encoder reranking via remote API or local GGUF model.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:98:- TRM evidence-gap detection (Z-score confidence check on score distribution).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:105:1. **Single Scoring Point.** All score modifications happen in Stage 2 (fusion) or Stage 3 (reranking). Stage 4 must not change any score field.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:116:- `@spec-kit/shared/contracts/retrieval-trace.ts` - Trace contract used for pipeline observability.

I have the hotspots now. I’m reading the exact handler and store ranges on both sides so the findings can stay implementation-backed: tool registration and search behavior in Engram, then ranking, routing, and disclosure behavior in Public.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go | sed -n '50,210p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    50	// ProfileAgent contains the tool names that AI agents need.
    51	// Sourced from actual skill files and memory protocol instructions
    52	// across all 4 supported agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Codex).
    53	var ProfileAgent = map[string]bool{
    54		"mem_save":              true, // proactive save — referenced 17 times across protocols
    55		"mem_search":            true, // search past memories — referenced 6 times
    56		"mem_context":           true, // recent context from previous sessions — referenced 10 times
    57		"mem_session_summary":   true, // end-of-session summary — referenced 16 times
    58		"mem_session_start":     true, // register session start
    59		"mem_session_end":       true, // mark session completed
    60		"mem_get_observation":   true, // full observation content after search — referenced 4 times
    61		"mem_suggest_topic_key": true, // stable topic key for upserts — referenced 3 times
    62		"mem_capture_passive":   true, // extract learnings from text — referenced in Gemini/Codex protocol
    63		"mem_save_prompt":       true, // save user prompts
    64		"mem_update":            true, // update observation by ID — skills say "use mem_update when you have an exact ID to correct"
    65	}
    66	
    67	// ProfileAdmin contains tools for TUI, dashboards, and manual curation
    68	// that are NOT referenced in any agent skill or memory protocol.
    69	var ProfileAdmin = map[string]bool{
    70		"mem_delete":         true, // only in OpenCode's ENGRAM_TOOLS filter, not in any agent instructions
    71		"mem_stats":          true, // only in OpenCode's ENGRAM_TOOLS filter, not in any agent instructions
    72		"mem_timeline":       true, // only in OpenCode's ENGRAM_TOOLS filter, not in any agent instructions
    73		"mem_merge_projects": true, // destructive curation tool — not for agent use
    74	}
    75	
    76	// Profiles maps profile names to their tool sets.
    77	var Profiles = map[string]map[string]bool{
    78		"agent": ProfileAgent,
    79		"admin": ProfileAdmin,
    80	}
    81	
    82	// ResolveTools takes a comma-separated string of profile names and/or
    83	// individual tool names and returns the set of tool names to register.
    84	// An empty input means "all" — every tool is registered.
    85	func ResolveTools(input string) map[string]bool {
    86		input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
    87		if input == "" || input == "all" {
    88			return nil // nil means register everything
    89		}
    90	
    91		result := make(map[string]bool)
    92		for _, token := range strings.Split(input, ",") {
    93			token = strings.TrimSpace(token)
    94			if token == "" {
    95				continue
    96			}
    97			if token == "all" {
    98				return nil
    99			}
   100			if profile, ok := Profiles[token]; ok {
   101				for tool := range profile {
   102					result[tool] = true
   103				}
   104			} else {
   105				// Treat as individual tool name
   106				result[token] = true
   107			}
   108		}
   109	
   110		if len(result) == 0 {
   111			return nil
   112		}
   113		return result
   114	}
   115	
   116	// NewServer creates an MCP server with ALL tools registered (backwards compatible).
   117	func NewServer(s *store.Store) *server.MCPServer {
   118		return NewServerWithConfig(s, MCPConfig{}, nil)
   119	}
   120	
   121	// serverInstructions tells MCP clients when to use Engram's tools.
   122	// 6 core tools are eager (always in context). The rest are deferred
   123	// and require ToolSearch to load.
   124	const serverInstructions = `Engram provides persistent memory that survives across sessions and compactions.
   125	
   126	CORE TOOLS (always available — use without ToolSearch):
   127	  mem_save — save decisions, bugs, discoveries, conventions PROACTIVELY (do not wait to be asked)
   128	  mem_search — find past work, decisions, or context from previous sessions
   129	  mem_context — get recent session history (call at session start or after compaction)
   130	  mem_session_summary — save end-of-session summary (MANDATORY before saying "done")
   131	  mem_get_observation — get full untruncated content of a search result by ID
   132	  mem_save_prompt — save user prompt for context
   133	
   134	DEFERRED TOOLS (use ToolSearch when needed):
   135	  mem_update, mem_suggest_topic_key, mem_session_start, mem_session_end,
   136	  mem_stats, mem_delete, mem_timeline, mem_capture_passive, mem_merge_projects
   137	
   138	PROACTIVE SAVE RULE: Call mem_save immediately after ANY decision, bug fix, discovery, or convention — not just when asked.`
   139	
   140	// NewServerWithTools creates an MCP server registering only the tools in
   141	// the allowlist. If allowlist is nil, all tools are registered.
   142	func NewServerWithTools(s *store.Store, allowlist map[string]bool) *server.MCPServer {
   143		return NewServerWithConfig(s, MCPConfig{}, allowlist)
   144	}
   145	
   146	// NewServerWithConfig creates an MCP server with full configuration including
   147	// default project detection and optional tool allowlist.
   148	func NewServerWithConfig(s *store.Store, cfg MCPConfig, allowlist map[string]bool) *server.MCPServer {
   149		srv := server.NewMCPServer(
   150			"engram",
   151			"0.1.0",
   152			server.WithToolCapabilities(true),
   153			server.WithInstructions(serverInstructions),
   154		)
   155	
   156		registerTools(srv, s, cfg, allowlist)
   157		return srv
   158	}
   159	
   160	// shouldRegister returns true if the tool should be registered given the
   161	// allowlist. If allowlist is nil, everything is allowed.
   162	func shouldRegister(name string, allowlist map[string]bool) bool {
   163		if allowlist == nil {
   164			return true
   165		}
   166		return allowlist[name]
   167	}
   168	
   169	func registerTools(srv *server.MCPServer, s *store.Store, cfg MCPConfig, allowlist map[string]bool) {
   170		// ─── mem_search (profile: agent, core — always in context) ─────────
   171		if shouldRegister("mem_search", allowlist) {
   172			srv.AddTool(
   173				mcp.NewTool("mem_search",
   174					mcp.WithDescription("Search your persistent memory across all sessions. Use this to find past decisions, bugs fixed, patterns used, files changed, or any context from previous coding sessions."),
   175					mcp.WithTitleAnnotation("Search Memory"),
   176					mcp.WithReadOnlyHintAnnotation(true),
   177					mcp.WithDestructiveHintAnnotation(false),
   178					mcp.WithIdempotentHintAnnotation(true),
   179					mcp.WithOpenWorldHintAnnotation(false),
   180					mcp.WithString("query",
   181						mcp.Required(),
   182						mcp.Description("Search query — natural language or keywords"),
   183					),
   184					mcp.WithString("type",
   185						mcp.Description("Filter by type: tool_use, file_change, command, file_read, search, manual, decision, architecture, bugfix, pattern"),
   186					),
   187					mcp.WithString("project",
   188						mcp.Description("Filter by project name"),
   189					),
   190					mcp.WithString("scope",
   191						mcp.Description("Filter by scope: project (default) or personal"),
   192					),
   193					mcp.WithNumber("limit",
   194						mcp.Description("Max results (default: 10, max: 20)"),
   195					),
   196				),
   197				handleSearch(s, cfg),
   198			)
   199		}
   200	
   201		// ─── mem_save (profile: agent, core — always in context) ───────────
   202		if shouldRegister("mem_save", allowlist) {

   623	// ─── Tool Handlers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   624	
   625	func handleSearch(s *store.Store, cfg MCPConfig) server.ToolHandlerFunc {
   626		return func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
   627			query, _ := req.GetArguments()["query"].(string)
   628			typ, _ := req.GetArguments()["type"].(string)
   629			project, _ := req.GetArguments()["project"].(string)
   630			scope, _ := req.GetArguments()["scope"].(string)
   631			limit := intArg(req, "limit", 10)
   632	
   633			// Apply default project when LLM sends empty
   634			if project == "" {
   635				project = cfg.DefaultProject
   636			}
   637			// Normalize project name
   638			project, _ = store.NormalizeProject(project)
   639	
   640			results, err := s.Search(query, store.SearchOptions{
   641				Type:    typ,
   642				Project: project,
   643				Scope:   scope,
   644				Limit:   limit,
   645			})
   646			if err != nil {
   647				return mcp.NewToolResultError(fmt.Sprintf("Search error: %s. Try simpler keywords.", err)), nil
   648			}
   649	
   650			if len(results) == 0 {
   651				return mcp.NewToolResultText(fmt.Sprintf("No memories found for: %q", query)), nil
   652			}
   653	
   654			var b strings.Builder
   655			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Found %d memories:\n\n", len(results))
   656			anyTruncated := false
   657			for i, r := range results {
   658				projectDisplay := ""
   659				if r.Project != nil {
   660					projectDisplay = fmt.Sprintf(" | project: %s", *r.Project)
   661				}
   662				preview := truncate(r.Content, 300)
   663				if len(r.Content) > 300 {
   664					anyTruncated = true
   665					preview += " [preview]"
   666				}
   667				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "[%d] #%d (%s) — %s\n    %s\n    %s%s | scope: %s\n\n",
   668					i+1, r.ID, r.Type, r.Title,
   669					preview,
   670					r.CreatedAt, projectDisplay, r.Scope)
   671			}
   672			if anyTruncated {
   673				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "---\nResults above are previews (300 chars). To read the full content of a specific memory, call mem_get_observation(id: <ID>).\n")
   674			}
   675	
   676			return mcp.NewToolResultText(b.String()), nil
   677		}
   678	}
   679	
   680	func handleSave(s *store.Store, cfg MCPConfig) server.ToolHandlerFunc {
   681		return func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
   682			title, _ := req.GetArguments()["title"].(string)
   683			content, _ := req.GetArguments()["content"].(string)
   684			typ, _ := req.GetArguments()["type"].(string)
   685			sessionID, _ := req.GetArguments()["session_id"].(string)
   686			project, _ := req.GetArguments()["project"].(string)
   687			scope, _ := req.GetArguments()["scope"].(string)
   688			topicKey, _ := req.GetArguments()["topic_key"].(string)
   689	
   690			// Apply default project when LLM sends empty

 succeeded in 0ms:
   940			ORDER BY created_at ASC
   941			LIMIT ?
   942		`
   943		return s.queryObservations(query, sessionID, limit)
   944	}
   945	
   946	// ─── Observations ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   947	
   948	func (s *Store) AddObservation(p AddObservationParams) (int64, error) {
   949		// Normalize project name (lowercase + trim) before any persistence
   950		p.Project, _ = NormalizeProject(p.Project)
   951	
   952		// Strip <private>...</private> tags before persisting ANYTHING
   953		title := stripPrivateTags(p.Title)
   954		content := stripPrivateTags(p.Content)
   955	
   956		if len(content) > s.cfg.MaxObservationLength {
   957			content = content[:s.cfg.MaxObservationLength] + "... [truncated]"
   958		}
   959		scope := normalizeScope(p.Scope)
   960		normHash := hashNormalized(content)
   961		topicKey := normalizeTopicKey(p.TopicKey)
   962	
   963		var observationID int64
   964		err := s.withTx(func(tx *sql.Tx) error {
   965			var obs *Observation
   966			if topicKey != "" {
   967				var existingID int64
   968				err := tx.QueryRow(
   969					`SELECT id FROM observations
   970					 WHERE topic_key = ?
   971					   AND ifnull(project, '') = ifnull(?, '')
   972					   AND scope = ?
   973					   AND deleted_at IS NULL
   974					 ORDER BY datetime(updated_at) DESC, datetime(created_at) DESC
   975					 LIMIT 1`,
   976					topicKey, nullableString(p.Project), scope,
   977				).Scan(&existingID)
   978				if err == nil {
   979					if _, err := s.execHook(tx,
   980						`UPDATE observations
   981						 SET type = ?,
   982						     title = ?,
   983						     content = ?,
   984						     tool_name = ?,
   985						     topic_key = ?,
   986						     normalized_hash = ?,
   987						     revision_count = revision_count + 1,
   988						     last_seen_at = datetime('now'),
   989						     updated_at = datetime('now')
   990						 WHERE id = ?`,
   991						p.Type,
   992						title,
   993						content,
   994						nullableString(p.ToolName),
   995						nullableString(topicKey),
   996						normHash,
   997						existingID,
   998					); err != nil {
   999						return err
  1000					}
  1001					obs, err = s.getObservationTx(tx, existingID)
  1002					if err != nil {
  1003						return err
  1004					}
  1005					observationID = existingID
  1006					return s.enqueueSyncMutationTx(tx, SyncEntityObservation, obs.SyncID, SyncOpUpsert, observationPayloadFromObservation(obs))
  1007				}
  1008				if err != sql.ErrNoRows {
  1009					return err
  1010				}
  1011			}
  1012	
  1013			window := dedupeWindowExpression(s.cfg.DedupeWindow)
  1014			var existingID int64
  1015			err := tx.QueryRow(
  1016				`SELECT id FROM observations
  1017				 WHERE normalized_hash = ?
  1018				   AND ifnull(project, '') = ifnull(?, '')
  1019				   AND scope = ?
  1020				   AND type = ?
  1021				   AND title = ?
  1022				   AND deleted_at IS NULL
  1023				   AND datetime(created_at) >= datetime('now', ?)
  1024				 ORDER BY created_at DESC
  1025				 LIMIT 1`,
  1026				normHash, nullableString(p.Project), scope, p.Type, title, window,
  1027			).Scan(&existingID)
  1028			if err == nil {
  1029				if _, err := s.execHook(tx,
  1030					`UPDATE observations
  1031					 SET duplicate_count = duplicate_count + 1,
  1032					     last_seen_at = datetime('now'),
  1033					     updated_at = datetime('now')
  1034					 WHERE id = ?`,
  1035					existingID,
  1036				); err != nil {
  1037					return err
  1038				}
  1039				obs, err = s.getObservationTx(tx, existingID)
  1040				if err != nil {
  1041					return err
  1042				}
  1043				observationID = existingID
  1044				return s.enqueueSyncMutationTx(tx, SyncEntityObservation, obs.SyncID, SyncOpUpsert, observationPayloadFromObservation(obs))
  1045			}
  1046			if err != sql.ErrNoRows {
  1047				return err
  1048			}
  1049	
  1050			syncID := newSyncID("obs")
  1051			res, err := s.execHook(tx,
  1052				`INSERT INTO observations (sync_id, session_id, type, title, content, tool_name, project, scope, topic_key, normalized_hash, revision_count, duplicate_count, last_seen_at, updated_at)
  1053				 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1, 1, datetime('now'), datetime('now'))`,
  1054				syncID, p.SessionID, p.Type, title, content,
  1055				nullableString(p.ToolName), nullableString(p.Project), scope, nullableString(topicKey), normHash,
  1056			)
  1057			if err != nil {
  1058				return err
  1059			}
  1060			observationID, err = res.LastInsertId()
  1061			if err != nil {
  1062				return err
  1063			}
  1064			obs, err = s.getObservationTx(tx, observationID)
  1065			if err != nil {
  1066				return err
  1067			}
  1068			return s.enqueueSyncMutationTx(tx, SyncEntityObservation, obs.SyncID, SyncOpUpsert, observationPayloadFromObservation(obs))
  1069		})
  1070		if err != nil {
  1071			return 0, err
  1072		}
  1073		return observationID, nil
  1074	}
  1075	
  1076	func (s *Store) RecentObservations(project, scope string, limit int) ([]Observation, error) {
  1077		// Normalize project filter for case-insensitive matching
  1078		project, _ = NormalizeProject(project)
  1079	
  1080		if limit <= 0 {
  1081			limit = s.cfg.MaxContextResults
  1082		}
  1083	
  1084		query := `
  1085			SELECT o.id, ifnull(o.sync_id, '') as sync_id, o.session_id, o.type, o.title, o.content, o.tool_name, o.project,
  1086			       o.scope, o.topic_key, o.revision_count, o.duplicate_count, o.last_seen_at, o.created_at, o.updated_at, o.deleted_at
  1087			FROM observations o
  1088			WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL
  1089		`
  1090		args := []any{}
  1091	
  1092		if project != "" {
  1093			query += " AND o.project = ?"
  1094			args = append(args, project)
  1095		}
  1096		if scope != "" {
  1097			query += " AND o.scope = ?"
  1098			args = append(args, normalizeScope(scope))
  1099		}
  1100	
  1101		query += " ORDER BY o.created_at DESC LIMIT ?"
  1102		args = append(args, limit)
  1103	
  1104		return s.queryObservations(query, args...)
  1105	}
  1106	
  1107	// ─── User Prompts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1108	
  1109	func (s *Store) AddPrompt(p AddPromptParams) (int64, error) {
  1110		// Normalize project name before storing
  1111		p.Project, _ = NormalizeProject(p.Project)
  1112	
  1113		content := stripPrivateTags(p.Content)
  1114		if len(content) > s.cfg.MaxObservationLength {
  1115			content = content[:s.cfg.MaxObservationLength] + "... [truncated]"
  1116		}
  1117	
  1118		var promptID int64
  1119		err := s.withTx(func(tx *sql.Tx) error {
  1120			syncID := newSyncID("prompt")
  1121			res, err := s.execHook(tx,
  1122				`INSERT INTO user_prompts (sync_id, session_id, content, project) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
  1123				syncID, p.SessionID, content, nullableString(p.Project),
  1124			)
  1125			if err != nil {
  1126				return err
  1127			}
  1128			promptID, err = res.LastInsertId()
  1129			if err != nil {
  1130				return err
  1131			}
  1132			return s.enqueueSyncMutationTx(tx, SyncEntityPrompt, syncID, SyncOpUpsert, syncPromptPayload{
  1133				SyncID:    syncID,
  1134				SessionID: p.SessionID,
  1135				Content:   content,
  1136				Project:   nullableString(p.Project),
  1137			})
  1138		})
  1139		if err != nil {
  1140			return 0, err
  1141		}
  1142		return promptID, nil
  1143	}
  1144	
  1145	func (s *Store) RecentPrompts(project string, limit int) ([]Prompt, error) {
  1146		// Normalize project filter for case-insensitive matching
  1147		project, _ = NormalizeProject(project)
  1148	
  1149		if limit <= 0 {
  1150			limit = 20
  1151		}
  1152	
  1153		query := `SELECT id, ifnull(sync_id, '') as sync_id, session_id, content, ifnull(project, '') as project, created_at FROM user_prompts`
  1154		args := []any{}
  1155	
  1156		if project != "" {
  1157			query += " WHERE project = ?"
  1158			args = append(args, project)
  1159		}
  1160	
  1161		query += " ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?"
  1162		args = append(args, limit)
  1163	
  1164		rows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, query, args...)
  1165		if err != nil {
  1166			return nil, err
  1167		}
  1168		defer rows.Close()
  1169	
  1170		var results []Prompt
  1171		for rows.Next() {
  1172			var p Prompt
  1173			if err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.SyncID, &p.SessionID, &p.Content, &p.Project, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
  1174				return nil, err
  1175			}
  1176			results = append(results, p)
  1177		}
  1178		return results, rows.Err()
  1179	}
  1180	
  1181	func (s *Store) SearchPrompts(query string, project string, limit int) ([]Prompt, error) {
  1182		if limit <= 0 {
  1183			limit = 10
  1184		}
  1185	
  1186		ftsQuery := sanitizeFTS(query)
  1187	
  1188		sql := `
  1189			SELECT p.id, ifnull(p.sync_id, '') as sync_id, p.session_id, p.content, ifnull(p.project, '') as project, p.created_at
  1190			FROM prompts_fts fts
  1191			JOIN user_prompts p ON p.id = fts.rowid
  1192			WHERE prompts_fts MATCH ?
  1193		`
  1194		args := []any{ftsQuery}
  1195	
  1196		if project != "" {
  1197			sql += " AND p.project = ?"
  1198			args = append(args, project)
  1199		}
  1200	
  1201		sql += " ORDER BY fts.rank LIMIT ?"
  1202		args = append(args, limit)
  1203	
  1204		rows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, sql, args...)
  1205		if err != nil {
  1206			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search prompts: %w", err)
  1207		}
  1208		defer rows.Close()
  1209	
  1210		var results []Prompt
  1211		for rows.Next() {
  1212			var p Prompt
  1213			if err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.SyncID, &p.SessionID, &p.Content, &p.Project, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
  1214				return nil, err
  1215			}
  1216			results = append(results, p)
  1217		}
  1218		return results, rows.Err()
  1219	}

  1224		row := s.db.QueryRow(
  1225			`SELECT id, ifnull(sync_id, '') as sync_id, session_id, type, title, content, tool_name, project,
