  1459	
  1460	// ─── Search (FTS5) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1461	
  1462	func (s *Store) Search(query string, opts SearchOptions) ([]SearchResult, error) {
  1463		// Normalize project filter so "Engram" finds records stored as "engram"
  1464		opts.Project, _ = NormalizeProject(opts.Project)
  1465	
  1466		limit := opts.Limit
  1467		if limit <= 0 {
  1468			limit = 10
  1469		}
  1470		if limit > s.cfg.MaxSearchResults {
  1471			limit = s.cfg.MaxSearchResults
  1472		}
  1473	
  1474		var directResults []SearchResult
  1475		if strings.Contains(query, "/") {
  1476			tkSQL := `
  1477				SELECT id, ifnull(sync_id, '') as sync_id, session_id, type, title, content, tool_name, project,
  1478				       scope, topic_key, revision_count, duplicate_count, last_seen_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
  1479				FROM observations
  1480				WHERE topic_key = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL
  1481			`
  1482			tkArgs := []any{query}
  1483	
  1484			if opts.Type != "" {
  1485				tkSQL += " AND type = ?"
  1486				tkArgs = append(tkArgs, opts.Type)
  1487			}
  1488			if opts.Project != "" {
  1489				tkSQL += " AND project = ?"
  1490				tkArgs = append(tkArgs, opts.Project)
  1491			}
  1492			if opts.Scope != "" {
  1493				tkSQL += " AND scope = ?"
  1494				tkArgs = append(tkArgs, normalizeScope(opts.Scope))
  1495			}
  1496	
  1497			tkSQL += " ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT ?"
  1498			tkArgs = append(tkArgs, limit)
  1499	
  1500			tkRows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, tkSQL, tkArgs...)
  1501			if err == nil {
  1502				defer tkRows.Close()
  1503				for tkRows.Next() {
  1504					var sr SearchResult
  1505					if err := tkRows.Scan(
  1506						&sr.ID, &sr.SyncID, &sr.SessionID, &sr.Type, &sr.Title, &sr.Content,
  1507						&sr.ToolName, &sr.Project, &sr.Scope, &sr.TopicKey, &sr.RevisionCount, &sr.DuplicateCount,
  1508						&sr.LastSeenAt, &sr.CreatedAt, &sr.UpdatedAt, &sr.DeletedAt,
  1509					); err != nil {
  1510						break
  1511					}
  1512					sr.Rank = -1000
  1513					directResults = append(directResults, sr)
  1514				}
  1515			}
  1516		}
  1517	
  1518		// Sanitize query for FTS5 — wrap each term in quotes to avoid syntax errors
  1519		ftsQuery := sanitizeFTS(query)
  1520	
  1521		sqlQ := `
  1522			SELECT o.id, ifnull(o.sync_id, '') as sync_id, o.session_id, o.type, o.title, o.content, o.tool_name, o.project,
  1523			       o.scope, o.topic_key, o.revision_count, o.duplicate_count, o.last_seen_at, o.created_at, o.updated_at, o.deleted_at,
  1524			       fts.rank
  1525			FROM observations_fts fts
  1526			JOIN observations o ON o.id = fts.rowid
  1527			WHERE observations_fts MATCH ? AND o.deleted_at IS NULL
  1528		`
  1529		args := []any{ftsQuery}
  1530	
  1531		if opts.Type != "" {
  1532			sqlQ += " AND o.type = ?"
  1533			args = append(args, opts.Type)
  1534		}
  1535	
  1536		if opts.Project != "" {
  1537			sqlQ += " AND o.project = ?"
  1538			args = append(args, opts.Project)
  1539		}
  1540	
  1541		if opts.Scope != "" {
  1542			sqlQ += " AND o.scope = ?"
  1543			args = append(args, normalizeScope(opts.Scope))
  1544		}
  1545	
  1546		sqlQ += " ORDER BY fts.rank LIMIT ?"
  1547		args = append(args, limit)
  1548	
  1549		rows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, sqlQ, args...)
  1550		if err != nil {
  1551			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search: %w", err)
  1552		}
  1553		defer rows.Close()
  1554	
  1555		seen := make(map[int64]bool)
  1556		for _, dr := range directResults {
  1557			seen[dr.ID] = true
  1558		}
  1559	
  1560		var results []SearchResult
  1561		results = append(results, directResults...)
  1562		for rows.Next() {
  1563			var sr SearchResult
  1564			if err := rows.Scan(
  1565				&sr.ID, &sr.SyncID, &sr.SessionID, &sr.Type, &sr.Title, &sr.Content,
  1566				&sr.ToolName, &sr.Project, &sr.Scope, &sr.TopicKey, &sr.RevisionCount, &sr.DuplicateCount,
  1567				&sr.LastSeenAt, &sr.CreatedAt, &sr.UpdatedAt, &sr.DeletedAt,
  1568				&sr.Rank,
  1569			); err != nil {
  1570				return nil, err
  1571			}
  1572			if !seen[sr.ID] {
  1573				results = append(results, sr)
  1574			}
  1575		}
  1576		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
  1577			return nil, err
  1578		}
  1579	
  1580		if len(results) > limit {
  1581			results = results[:limit]
  1582		}
  1583		return results, nil
  1584	}
  1585	
  1586	// ─── Stats ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1587	
  1588	func (s *Store) Stats() (*Stats, error) {
  1589		stats := &Stats{}
  1590	
  1591		s.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions").Scan(&stats.TotalSessions)
  1592		s.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM observations WHERE deleted_at IS NULL").Scan(&stats.TotalObservations)
  1593		s.db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_prompts").Scan(&stats.TotalPrompts)
  1594	
  1595		rows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db, "SELECT project FROM observations WHERE project IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY project ORDER BY MAX(created_at) DESC")
  1596		if err != nil {
  1597			return stats, nil
  1598		}
  1599		defer rows.Close()
  1600	
  1601		for rows.Next() {
  1602			var p string
  1603			if err := rows.Scan(&p); err == nil {
  1604				stats.Projects = append(stats.Projects, p)
  1605			}
  1606		}
  1607	
  1608		return stats, nil
  1609	}
  1610	
  1611	// ─── Context Formatting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1612	
  1613	func (s *Store) FormatContext(project, scope string) (string, error) {
  1614		sessions, err := s.RecentSessions(project, 5)
  1615		if err != nil {
  1616			return "", err
  1617		}
  1618	
  1619		observations, err := s.RecentObservations(project, scope, s.cfg.MaxContextResults)
  1620		if err != nil {
  1621			return "", err
  1622		}
  1623	
  1624		prompts, err := s.RecentPrompts(project, 10)
  1625		if err != nil {
  1626			return "", err
  1627		}
  1628	
  1629		if len(sessions) == 0 && len(observations) == 0 && len(prompts) == 0 {
  1630			return "", nil
  1631		}
  1632	
  1633		var b strings.Builder
  1634		b.WriteString("## Memory from Previous Sessions\n\n")
  1635	
  1636		if len(sessions) > 0 {
  1637			b.WriteString("### Recent Sessions\n")
  1638			for _, sess := range sessions {
  1639				summary := ""
  1640				if sess.Summary != nil {
  1641					summary = fmt.Sprintf(": %s", truncate(*sess.Summary, 200))
  1642				}
  1643				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- **%s** (%s)%s [%d observations]\n",
  1644					sess.Project, sess.StartedAt, summary, sess.ObservationCount)
  1645			}
  1646			b.WriteString("\n")
  1647		}
  1648	
  1649		if len(prompts) > 0 {
  1650			b.WriteString("### Recent User Prompts\n")
  1651			for _, p := range prompts {
  1652				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s: %s\n", p.CreatedAt, truncate(p.Content, 200))
  1653			}
  1654			b.WriteString("\n")
  1655		}
  1656	
  1657		if len(observations) > 0 {
  1658			b.WriteString("### Recent Observations\n")
  1659			for _, obs := range observations {
  1660				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- [%s] **%s**: %s\n",
  1661					obs.Type, obs.Title, truncate(obs.Content, 300))
  1662			}
  1663			b.WriteString("\n")
  1664		}
  1665	
  1666		return b.String(), nil
  1667	}
  1668	
  1669	// ─── Export / Import ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1670	
  1671	func (s *Store) Export() (*ExportData, error) {
  1672		data := &ExportData{
  1673			Version:    "0.1.0",
  1674			ExportedAt: Now(),
  1675		}
  1676	
  1677		// Sessions
  1678		rows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db,
  1679			"SELECT id, project, directory, started_at, ended_at, summary FROM sessions ORDER BY started_at",
  1680		)
  1681		if err != nil {
  1682			return nil, fmt.Errorf("export sessions: %w", err)
  1683		}
  1684		defer rows.Close()
  1685		for rows.Next() {
  1686			var sess Session
  1687			if err := rows.Scan(&sess.ID, &sess.Project, &sess.Directory, &sess.StartedAt, &sess.EndedAt, &sess.Summary); err != nil {
  1688				return nil, err
  1689			}
  1690			data.Sessions = append(data.Sessions, sess)
  1691		}
  1692		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
  1693			return nil, err
  1694		}
  1695	
  1696		// Observations
  1697		obsRows, err := s.queryItHook(s.db,
  1698			`SELECT id, ifnull(sync_id, '') as sync_id, session_id, type, title, content, tool_name, project,
  1699			        scope, topic_key, revision_count, duplicate_count, last_seen_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
  1700			 FROM observations ORDER BY id`,
  1701		)
  1702		if err != nil {
  1703			return nil, fmt.Errorf("export observations: %w", err)
  1704		}
  1705		defer obsRows.Close()

 succeeded in 0ms:
  3190			n = strings.ReplaceAll(n, "__", "_")
  3191		}
  3192		if n == project {
  3193			return n, ""
  3194		}
  3195		return n, fmt.Sprintf("⚠️ Project name normalized: %q → %q", project, n)
  3196	}
  3197	
  3198	// SuggestTopicKey generates a stable topic key suggestion from type/title/content.
  3199	// It infers a topic family (e.g. architecture/*, bug/*) and then appends
  3200	// a normalized segment from title/content for stable cross-session keys.
  3201	func SuggestTopicKey(typ, title, content string) string {
  3202		family := inferTopicFamily(typ, title, content)
  3203		cleanTitle := stripPrivateTags(title)
  3204		segment := normalizeTopicSegment(cleanTitle)
  3205	
  3206		if segment == "" {
  3207			cleanContent := stripPrivateTags(content)
  3208			words := strings.Fields(strings.ToLower(cleanContent))
  3209			if len(words) > 8 {
  3210				words = words[:8]
  3211			}
  3212			segment = normalizeTopicSegment(strings.Join(words, " "))
  3213		}
  3214	
  3215		if segment == "" {
  3216			segment = "general"
  3217		}
  3218	
  3219		if strings.HasPrefix(segment, family+"-") {
  3220			segment = strings.TrimPrefix(segment, family+"-")
  3221		}
  3222		if segment == "" || segment == family {
  3223			segment = "general"
  3224		}
  3225	
  3226		return family + "/" + segment
  3227	}
  3228	
  3229	func inferTopicFamily(typ, title, content string) string {
  3230		t := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(typ))
  3231		switch t {
  3232		case "architecture", "design", "adr", "refactor":
  3233			return "architecture"
  3234		case "bug", "bugfix", "fix", "incident", "hotfix":
  3235			return "bug"
  3236		case "decision":
  3237			return "decision"
  3238		case "pattern", "convention", "guideline":
  3239			return "pattern"
  3240		case "config", "setup", "infra", "infrastructure", "ci":
  3241			return "config"
  3242		case "discovery", "investigation", "root_cause", "root-cause":
  3243			return "discovery"
  3244		case "learning", "learn":
  3245			return "learning"
  3246		case "session_summary":
  3247			return "session"
  3248		}
  3249	
  3250		text := strings.ToLower(title + " " + content)
  3251		if hasAny(text, "bug", "fix", "panic", "error", "crash", "regression", "incident", "hotfix") {
  3252			return "bug"
  3253		}
  3254		if hasAny(text, "architecture", "design", "adr", "boundary", "hexagonal", "refactor") {
  3255			return "architecture"
  3256		}
  3257		if hasAny(text, "decision", "tradeoff", "chose", "choose", "decide") {
  3258			return "decision"
  3259		}
  3260		if hasAny(text, "pattern", "convention", "naming", "guideline") {
  3261			return "pattern"
  3262		}
  3263		if hasAny(text, "config", "setup", "environment", "env", "docker", "pipeline") {
  3264			return "config"
  3265		}
  3266		if hasAny(text, "discovery", "investigate", "investigation", "found", "root cause") {
  3267			return "discovery"
  3268		}
  3269		if hasAny(text, "learned", "learning") {
  3270			return "learning"
  3271		}
  3272	
  3273		if t != "" && t != "manual" {
  3274			return normalizeTopicSegment(t)
  3275		}
  3276	
  3277		return "topic"
  3278	}
  3279	
  3280	func hasAny(text string, words ...string) bool {
  3281		for _, w := range words {
  3282			if strings.Contains(text, w) {
  3283				return true
  3284			}
  3285		}
  3286		return false
  3287	}
  3288	
  3289	func normalizeTopicSegment(s string) string {
  3290		v := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(s))
  3291		if v == "" {
  3292			return ""
  3293		}
  3294		re := regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-z0-9]+`)
  3295		v = re.ReplaceAllString(v, " ")
  3296		v = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), "-")
  3297		if len(v) > 100 {
  3298			v = v[:100]
  3299		}
  3300		return v
  3301	}
  3302	
  3303	func normalizeTopicKey(topic string) string {
  3304		v := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(topic))
  3305		if v == "" {
  3306			return ""
  3307		}
  3308		v = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), "-")
  3309		if len(v) > 120 {
  3310			v = v[:120]
  3311		}
  3312		return v
  3313	}
  3314	
  3315	func derefString(v *string) string {
  3316		if v == nil {
  3317			return ""
  3318		}
  3319		return *v
  3320	}
  3321	
  3322	func hashNormalized(content string) string {
  3323		normalized := strings.ToLower(strings.Join(strings.Fields(content), " "))
  3324		h := sha256.Sum256([]byte(normalized))
  3325		return hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
  3326	}
  3327	
  3328	func dedupeWindowExpression(window time.Duration) string {
  3329		if window <= 0 {
  3330			window = 15 * time.Minute
  3331		}
  3332		minutes := int(window.Minutes())
  3333		if minutes < 1 {
  3334			minutes = 1
  3335		}
  3336		return "-" + strconv.Itoa(minutes) + " minutes"
  3337	}
  3338	
  3339	func maxInt(a, b int) int {
  3340		if a > b {
  3341			return a
  3342		}
  3343		return b
  3344	}
  3345	
  3346	func normalizeSyncTargetKey(targetKey string) string {
  3347		if strings.TrimSpace(targetKey) == "" {
  3348			return DefaultSyncTargetKey
  3349		}
  3350		return strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(targetKey))
  3351	}
  3352	
  3353	func newSyncID(prefix string) string {
  3354		b := make([]byte, 8)
  3355		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
  3356			return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d", prefix, time.Now().UTC().UnixNano())
  3357		}
  3358		return prefix + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(b)
  3359	}
  3360	
  3361	func normalizeExistingSyncID(existing, prefix string) string {
  3362		if strings.TrimSpace(existing) != "" {
  3363			return existing
  3364		}
  3365		return newSyncID(prefix)
  3366	}
  3367	
  3368	// privateTagRegex matches <private>...</private> tags and their contents.
  3369	// Supports multiline and nested content. Case-insensitive.
  3370	var privateTagRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`(?is)<private>.*?</private>`)
  3371	
  3372	// stripPrivateTags removes all <private>...</private> content from a string.
  3373	// This ensures sensitive information (API keys, passwords, personal data)
  3374	// is never persisted to the memory database.
  3375	func stripPrivateTags(s string) string {
  3376		result := privateTagRegex.ReplaceAllString(s, "[REDACTED]")
  3377		// Clean up multiple consecutive [REDACTED] and excessive whitespace
  3378		result = strings.TrimSpace(result)
  3379		return result
  3380	}
  3381	
  3382	// sanitizeFTS wraps each word in quotes so FTS5 doesn't choke on special chars.
  3383	// "fix auth bug" → `"fix" "auth" "bug"`
  3384	func sanitizeFTS(query string) string {
  3385		words := strings.Fields(query)
  3386		for i, w := range words {
  3387			// Strip existing quotes to avoid double-quoting
  3388			w = strings.Trim(w, `"`)
  3389			words[i] = `"` + w + `"`
  3390		}
  3391		return strings.Join(words, " ")
  3392	}
  3393	
  3394	// ─── Passive Capture ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  3395	
  3396	// PassiveCaptureParams holds the input for passive memory capture.
  3397	type PassiveCaptureParams struct {
  3398		SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
  3399		Content   string `json:"content"`
  3400		Project   string `json:"project,omitempty"`
  3401		Source    string `json:"source,omitempty"` // e.g. "subagent-stop", "session-end"
  3402	}
  3403	
  3404	// PassiveCaptureResult holds the output of passive memory capture.
  3405	type PassiveCaptureResult struct {
  3406		Extracted  int `json:"extracted"`  // Total learnings found in text
  3407		Saved      int `json:"saved"`      // New observations created
  3408		Duplicates int `json:"duplicates"` // Skipped because already existed
  3409	}
  3410	
  3411	// learningHeaderPattern matches section headers for learnings in both English and Spanish.
  3412	var learningHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(
  3413		`(?im)^#{2,3}\s+(?:Aprendizajes(?:\s+Clave)?|Key\s+Learnings?|Learnings?):?\s*$`,
  3414	)
  3415

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md | sed -n '60,110p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    60	| `mem_search` | Full-text search across all memories |
    61	| `mem_session_summary` | Save end-of-session summary |
    62	| `mem_context` | Get recent context from previous sessions |
    63	| `mem_timeline` | Chronological context around a specific observation |
    64	| `mem_get_observation` | Get full content of a specific memory |
    65	| `mem_save_prompt` | Save a user prompt for future context |
    66	| `mem_stats` | Memory system statistics |
    67	| `mem_session_start` | Register a session start |
    68	| `mem_session_end` | Mark a session as completed |
    69	| `mem_capture_passive` | Extract learnings from text output |
    70	| `mem_merge_projects` | Merge project name variants into canonical name (admin) |
    71	
    72	---
    73	
    74	## Progressive Disclosure (3-Layer Pattern)
    75	
    76	Token-efficient memory retrieval — don't dump everything, drill in:
    77	
    78	```
    79	1. mem_search "auth middleware"     → compact results with IDs (~100 tokens each)
    80	2. mem_timeline observation_id=42  → what happened before/after in that session
    81	3. mem_get_observation id=42       → full untruncated content
    82	```
    83	
    84	---
    85	
    86	## Memory Hygiene
    87	
    88	- `mem_save` now supports `scope` (`project` default, `personal` optional)
    89	- `mem_save` also supports `topic_key`; with a topic key, saves become upserts (same project+scope+topic updates the existing memory)
    90	- Exact dedupe prevents repeated inserts in a rolling window (hash + project + scope + type + title)
    91	- Duplicates update metadata (`duplicate_count`, `last_seen_at`, `updated_at`) instead of creating new rows
    92	- Topic upserts increment `revision_count` so evolving decisions stay in one memory
    93	- `mem_delete` uses soft-delete by default (`deleted_at`), with optional hard delete
    94	- `mem_search`, `mem_context`, recent lists, and timeline ignore soft-deleted observations
    95	
    96	---
    97	
    98	## Topic Key Workflow (Recommended)
    99	
   100	Use this when a topic evolves over time (architecture, long-running feature decisions, etc.):
   101	
   102	```text
   103	1. mem_suggest_topic_key(type="architecture", title="Auth architecture")
   104	2. mem_save(..., topic_key="architecture-auth-architecture")
   105	3. Later change on same topic -> mem_save(..., same topic_key)
   106	   => existing observation is updated (revision_count++)
   107	```
   108	
   109	Different topics should use different keys (e.g. `architecture/auth-model` vs `bug/auth-nil-panic`) so they never overwrite each other.
   110

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/README.md:42:Code graph handlers (`code-graph/` subdirectory):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/README.md:44:- `code-graph/scan.ts` - `code_graph_scan`: index workspace files, build structural graph.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/README.md:45:- `code-graph/query.ts` - `code_graph_query`: query structural relationships (outline, calls, imports).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/README.md:46:- `code-graph/status.ts` - `code_graph_status`: report graph health and statistics.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/README.md:47:- `code-graph/context.ts` - `code_graph_context`: LLM-oriented compact graph neighborhoods.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/README.md:70:- `memory-index.ts` and `mutation-hooks.ts` work together so index, update, and stale-delete flows clear trigger, constitutional, graph, co-activation, and degree caches.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/README.md:80:- Post-mutation invalidation clears `clearDegreeCache()` alongside trigger and constitutional caches so graph-derived retrieval signals cannot serve stale data after mutations.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:7:  - "vector search"
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:12:> 5-channel hybrid search architecture combining vector, lexical (BM25/FTS5), graph-based and structure-aware graph retrieval, fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and Adaptive Fusion.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:24:  - [GRAPH SIGNAL FEATURES](#graph-signal-features)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:27:- [FTS CAPABILITY CASCADE FLOOR](#fts-capability-cascade-floor)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:75:`vector-index.ts` is the primary typed export surface for the vector index and re-exports the split schema, query, mutation, store and alias modules. `vector-index-impl.ts` is now a 14-line backward-compatibility shim that simply re-exports `vector-index.ts` for older import paths.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:93:| Vector | `vector-index.ts` | Semantic similarity via sqlite-vec through the split vector-index modules |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:94:| BM25 | `bm25-index.ts` | Pure TypeScript keyword matching |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:95:| FTS5 | `sqlite-fts.ts` | SQLite FTS5 BM25 weighted scoring |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:96:| Graph | `graph-search-fn.ts` | Causal edge traversal + typed-weighted degree (R4) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:97:| Degree | `graph-search-fn.ts` | Structural graph discovery via typed-weighted degree |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:105:2. Causal boost — graph-traversal neighbor amplification
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:172:<a id="fts-capability-cascade-floor"></a>
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:175:Packet `026-graph-and-context-optimization/010-fts-capability-cascade-floor` freezes the lexical capability contract that packet `002-implement-cache-warning-hooks` now consumes. `memory_search` responses expose:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:177:- `lexicalPath`: the lane that actually ran for lexical retrieval. Current packet-owned values are `fts5` and `unavailable`. The wider response schema also allows `like`, but packet `010` does not claim that lane for this runtime seam.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:184:| `ok` | FTS5 compile probe passed, `memory_fts` exists, and BM25 ranking executed normally | `fts5` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:186:| `missing_table` | FTS5 support is present, but `memory_fts` is missing at runtime | `unavailable` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:187:| `no_such_module_fts5` | The SQLite engine rejects FTS5 usage with `no such module: fts5` | `unavailable` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:188:| `bm25_runtime_failure` | The FTS5 table exists, but the `bm25(...)` ranking call fails at runtime | `unavailable` |

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:636:<a id="graph-signal-features"></a>
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:641:**R4: Typed-Weighted Degree** (`graph-search-fn.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:658:**N2a: Graph Momentum** (`graph-signals.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:661:**N2b: Causal Depth** (`graph-signals.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:662:The causal graph is condensed into strongly connected components, then longest-path depth is computed across the resulting DAG. Shortcut edges do not collapse deeper chains; cycle members share one bounded depth layer, and the final depth is normalized by the deepest reachable component chain (`maxDepth`). Score bonus: `normalizedDepth * 0.05` — rewards structurally deep nodes in the causal chain.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:665:BFS connected-component labelling assigns community IDs. When the largest component contains >50% of all nodes, escalates to Louvain modularity optimization for finer-grained communities. Community co-members are injected into Stage 2 results before graph signal scoring. Gated via `SPECKIT_COMMUNITY_DETECTION`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:668:Measures graph density and reports metrics used for R10 entity extraction escalation decisions.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:718:**Lexical Normalization + BM25 Document Text** (`bm25-index.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:736:**Degree Cache Invalidation** (`graph-search-fn.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:737:Typed-degree ranking is cached per database connection and invalidated through `clearDegreeCache()` / `clearDegreeCacheForDb()` after causal-edge and mutation flows, so the degree channel stays in sync with recent graph writes.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:34:The `pipeline/` directory implements the core retrieval pipeline behind `memory_search`. Each search request flows through four sequential stages, each with a defined I/O contract and clear responsibility boundary. The pipeline supports hybrid, vector and multi-concept search types with optional deep-mode query expansion, cross-encoder reranking, MMR diversity pruning and MPAB chunk-to-parent reassembly.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:46:| `stage1-candidate-gen.ts` | Stage 1: Candidate Generation. Runs search channels (hybrid, vector, multi-concept), applies deep-mode query expansion (R6), embedding-based expansion (R12), summary embeddings (R8), constitutional memory injection, quality threshold filtering and tier/contextType filtering. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:47:| `stage2-fusion.ts` | Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration. The single authoritative scoring point. Applies 9 signal steps in fixed order: session boost, causal boost, co-activation spreading, community co-retrieval, graph signals, FSRS testing effect, intent weights (non-hybrid only, G2 prevention), artifact routing, feedback signals, artifact limiting, anchor metadata and validation metadata scoring. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:78:- Channels: hybrid (with optional deep-mode expansion), vector, multi-concept.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:108:4. **Score Resolution Consistency.** All stages use the shared `resolveEffectiveScore()` function from `types.ts` with fallback chain: `intentAdjustedScore` > `rrfScore` > `score` > `similarity/100`, clamped to [0, 1].
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:114:- `mcp_server/lib/search/` - Parent search directory containing hybrid search, vector index, cross-encoder and other search modules consumed by the pipeline.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts | sed -n '480,760p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   480	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   481	
   482	/** Handle memory_search tool — performs hybrid vector/BM25 search with intent-aware ranking.
   483	 * @param args - Search arguments (query, concepts, mode, specFolder, etc.)
   484	 * @returns MCP response with ranked search results
   485	 */
   486	async function handleMemorySearch(args: SearchArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   487	  const _searchStartTime = Date.now();
   488	  resetLastLexicalCapabilitySnapshot();
   489	  // BUG-001: Check for external database updates before processing
   490	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
   491	
   492	  const {
   493	    cursor,
   494	    query,
   495	    concepts,
   496	    specFolder,
   497	    folderBoost,
   498	    tenantId,
   499	    userId,
   500	    agentId,
   501	    sharedSpaceId,
   502	    limit: rawLimit = 10,
   503	    tier,
   504	    contextType,
   505	    useDecay: useDecay = true,
   506	    includeContiguity: includeContiguity = false,
   507	    includeConstitutional: includeConstitutional = true,
   508	    includeContent: includeContent = false,
   509	    anchors,
   510	    bypassCache: bypassCache = false,
   511	    sessionId,
   512	    enableDedup: enableDedup = true,
   513	    intent: explicitIntent,
   514	    autoDetectIntent: autoDetectIntent = true,
   515	    minState,  // No default — memoryState column not yet in schema; defaulting to 'WARM' filters all rows
   516	    applyStateLimits: applyStateLimits = false,
   517	    rerank = true, // Enable reranking by default for better result quality
   518	    applyLengthPenalty: applyLengthPenalty = true,
   519	    trackAccess: trackAccess = false, // opt-in, off by default
   520	    includeArchived: includeArchived = false, // REQ-206: exclude archived by default
   521	    enableSessionBoost: enableSessionBoost = isSessionBoostEnabled(),
   522	    enableCausalBoost: enableCausalBoost = isCausalBoostEnabled(),
   523	    minQualityScore,
   524	    min_quality_score,
   525	    mode,
   526	    includeTrace: includeTraceArg = false,
   527	    sessionTransition,
   528	    profile,
   529	    retrievalLevel: retrievalLevel = 'auto',
   530	  } = args;
   531	  const includeTraceByFlag = process.env.SPECKIT_RESPONSE_TRACE === 'true';

   828	            // Fetch the actual memory rows for community member IDs
   829	            const memberIds = communityResults.totalMemberIds.slice(0, 20);
   830	            const placeholders = memberIds.map(() => '?').join(', ');
   831	            const db = requireDb();
   832	            const memberRows = db.prepare(`
   833	              SELECT id, title, similarity, content, file_path, importance_tier, context_type,
   834	                     quality_score, created_at

   838	
   839	            if (memberRows.length > 0) {

   874	        }
   875	      }

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md | sed -n '34,90p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    34	The `pipeline/` directory implements the core retrieval pipeline behind `memory_search`. Each search request flows through four sequential stages, each with a defined I/O contract and clear responsibility boundary. The pipeline supports hybrid, vector and multi-concept search types with optional deep-mode query expansion, cross-encoder reranking, MMR diversity pruning and MPAB chunk-to-parent reassembly.
    35	
    36	The public API is a single function: `executePipeline(config)` exported from `index.ts`.
    37	
    38	---
    39	
    40	## 2. STRUCTURE
    41	
    42	| File | Description |
    43	|------|-------------|
    44	| `index.ts` | Public barrel export. Re-exports `executePipeline` from the orchestrator and all pipeline type definitions from `types.ts`. |
    45	| `orchestrator.ts` | Wires the four stages together in sequence. Passes each stage's output as input to the next and assembles the final `PipelineResult` with per-stage metadata. |
    46	| `stage1-candidate-gen.ts` | Stage 1: Candidate Generation. Runs search channels (hybrid, vector, multi-concept), applies deep-mode query expansion (R6), embedding-based expansion (R12), summary embeddings (R8), constitutional memory injection, quality threshold filtering and tier/contextType filtering. |
    47	| `stage2-fusion.ts` | Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration. The single authoritative scoring point. Applies 9 signal steps in fixed order: session boost, causal boost, co-activation spreading, community co-retrieval, graph signals, FSRS testing effect, intent weights (non-hybrid only, G2 prevention), artifact routing, feedback signals, artifact limiting, anchor metadata and validation metadata scoring. |
    48	| `stage3-rerank.ts` | Stage 3: Rerank + Aggregate. Applies cross-encoder reranking (remote or local GGUF), MMR diversity pruning and MPAB chunk collapse with parent document reassembly from the database. |
    49	| `stage4-filter.ts` | Stage 4: Filter + Annotate. Removes results below minimum memory state priority, enforces per-tier limits, runs TRM evidence-gap detection and attaches annotation metadata. Enforces the score immutability invariant at runtime. |
    50	| `types.ts` | All pipeline data contracts: `PipelineRow`, `Stage4ReadonlyRow`, `PipelineConfig`, stage I/O interfaces (`Stage1Input`/`Output` through `Stage4Input`/`Output`), `PipelineResult`, `ScoreSnapshot` and the `resolveEffectiveScore()` shared score resolution function. Also provides `captureScoreSnapshot()` and `verifyScoreInvariant()` for Stage 4 defence-in-depth. |
    51	
    52	---
    53	
    54	## 3. PIPELINE STAGES
    55	
    56	```text
    57	Query + Config
    58	      |
    59	      v
    60	  Stage 1: Candidate Generation
    61	      |  search channels, constitutional injection, quality filter
    62	      v
    63	  Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration
    64	      |  session/causal/co-activation boosts, intent weights,
    65	      |  artifact routing, feedback signals, FSRS write-back
    66	      v
    67	  Stage 3: Rerank + Aggregate
    68	      |  cross-encoder reranking, MMR diversity, MPAB chunk collapse
    69	      v
    70	  Stage 4: Filter + Annotate
    71	      |  state filter, per-tier limits, TRM evidence gap, annotations
    72	      v
    73	  PipelineResult { results, metadata, annotations, trace }
    74	```
    75	
    76	**Stage 1 - Candidate Generation** (`stage1-candidate-gen.ts`)
    77	- Runs one or more search channels based on `searchType` and `mode`.
    78	- Channels: hybrid (with optional deep-mode expansion), vector, multi-concept.
    79	- Optional R12 embedding expansion and R8 summary embedding channels.
    80	- Injects constitutional memories when absent and no tier filter is active.
    81	- Applies quality threshold and tier/contextType filters.
    82	
    83	**Stage 2 - Fusion + Signal Integration** (`stage2-fusion.ts`)
    84	- The only stage where scoring modifications happen (except Stage 3 reranking).
    85	- 9 signal steps applied in a fixed order that must not be reordered.
    86	- G2 prevention: intent weights are applied only for non-hybrid search types.
    87	- FSRS testing effect fires only when `trackAccess` is explicitly true.
    88	
    89	**Stage 3 - Rerank + Aggregate** (`stage3-rerank.ts`)
    90	- Cross-encoder reranking via remote API or local GGUF model.

   848	 * Run multi-channel hybrid search combining vector, FTS, BM25, and graph results with per-source normalization.
   849	 * Prefer hybridSearchEnhanced() or searchWithFallback() instead. This function uses naive per-source
   850	 * min-max normalization which produces different orderings than the RRF pipeline in hybridSearchEnhanced().
   851	 * Retained as internal fallback only.
   852	 */
   853	async function hybridSearch(
   854	  query: string,
   855	  embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
   856	  options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
   857	): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
   858	  const {
   859	    limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT,
   860	    specFolder,
   861	    minSimilarity = 0,
   862	    useBm25 = isBm25Enabled(),
   863	    useFts = true,
   864	    useVector = true,
   865	    useGraph = true,
   866	    includeArchived = false,
   867	  } = options;
   868	
   869	  const results: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
   870	
   871	  // Vector search
   872	  if (useVector && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
   873	    try {
   874	      const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
   875	        limit,
   876	        specFolder,
   877	        minSimilarity,
   878	        includeConstitutional: false, // Handler manages constitutional separately
   879	        includeArchived,
   880	      });
   881	      for (const r of vectorResults) {
   882	        results.push({
   883	          ...r,
   884	          id: r.id as number,
   885	          score: (r.similarity as number) || 0,
   886	          source: 'vector',
   887	        });
   888	      }
   889	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   890	      const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   891	      console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Vector search failed: ${msg}`);
   892	    }
   893	  }
   894	
   895	  // FTS search
   896	  if (useFts) {
   897	    const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
   898	    results.push(...ftsResults);
   899	  }
   900	
   901	  // BM25 search
   902	  if (useBm25) {
   903	    const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, { limit, specFolder });
   904	    results.push(...bm25Results);
   905	  }
   906	
   907	  // Graph search
   908	  if (useGraph && graphSearchFn) {
   909	    try {
   910	      const graphResults = graphSearchFn(query, { limit, specFolder });
   911	      for (const r of graphResults) {
   912	        results.push({
   913	          ...r,
   914	          id: r.id as number | string,
   915	          score: (r.score as number) || 0,
   916	          source: 'graph',
   917	        });
   918	      }
   919	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   920	      const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   921	      console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Graph search failed: ${msg}`);
   922	    }
   923	  }
   924	
   925	  // Normalize scores per source before merging so one method's raw scale
   926	  // Does not dominate others during final ranking.
   927	  const bySource = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult[]>();
   928	  for (const r of results) {
   929	    const src = r.source || 'unknown';
   930	    if (!bySource.has(src)) bySource.set(src, []);
   931	    bySource.get(src)!.push(r); // non-null safe: has() guard above guarantees entry exists
   932	  }
   933	
   934	  const normalized: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
   935	  for (const [, group] of bySource) {
   936	    if (group.length === 0) continue;
   937	    const scores = group.map(r => r.score);
   938	    let min = Infinity;
   939	    let max = -Infinity;
   940	    for (const s of scores) {
   941	      if (s < min) min = s;
   942	      if (s > max) max = s;
   943	    }
   944	    const range = max - min;
   945	    for (const r of group) {
   946	      normalized.push({
   947	        ...r,
   948	        score: range > 0 ? (r.score - min) / range : (r.score > 0 ? 1.0 : 0),
   949	      });
   950	    }
   951	  }
   952	
   953	  // Deduplicate by ID (keep highest normalized score)
   954	  // LIMITATION (P1-1): When a result appears in multiple sources (e.g., vector + fts),
   955	  // Only the highest-scoring entry's `source` is preserved. Multi-source provenance
   956	  // Is lost here. To fix properly, HybridSearchResult would need a `sources: string[]`
   957	  // Field and downstream consumers would need to be updated accordingly.
   958	  const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
   959	  for (const r of normalized) {
   960	    const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
   961	    const existing = deduped.get(canonicalId);
   962	    if (!existing || r.score > existing.score) {
   963	      deduped.set(canonicalId, r);
   964	    }
   965	  }
   966	
   967	  return Array.from(deduped.values())
   968	    .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
   969	    .slice(0, limit);
   970	}
   971	
   972	/**
   973	 * Enhanced hybrid search with RRF fusion.
   974	 * All search channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
   975	 * is correct — Promise.all would add overhead without achieving parallelism.
   976	 */
   977	async function hybridSearchEnhanced(
   978	  query: string,
   979	  embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
   980	  options: HybridSearchOptions = {}

   184	/** Handle memory_match_triggers tool - matches prompt against trigger phrases with cognitive decay */
   185	async function handleMemoryMatchTriggers(args: TriggerArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   186	  const {
   187	    prompt,
   188	    limit: rawLimit = 3,
   189	    session_id: rawSessionId,
   190	    turnNumber: rawTurnNumber = 1,
   191	    include_cognitive: includeCognitive = true
   192	  } = args;
   193	
   194	  // Validate inputs before any I/O (checkDatabaseUpdated is deferred until after validation)
   195	  if (!prompt || typeof prompt !== 'string') {
   196	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   197	      tool: 'memory_match_triggers',
   198	      error: 'prompt is required and must be a string',
   199	      code: 'E_VALIDATION',
   200	      details: { parameter: 'prompt' },

codex
The retrieval picture is getting clear: Engram is deliberately small and lexical, while Public is running a routed multi-channel retrieval system with stronger scope controls and code-search separation. I’m pulling one more set of line-level evidence around progressive disclosure and topic-key-like retrieval hooks so the recommendations stay concrete.

   433	  results: DisclosureResult[],
   434	  pageSize: number = DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE,
   435	  query: string = '',

   437	): ProgressiveResponse {
   438	  if (!Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) {
   439	    return {
   440	      summaryLayer: { count: 0, digest: '0 results' },
   441	      results: [],
   442	      continuation: null,
   443	    };
   444	  }
   445	
   446	  // When feature flag is OFF, return all results as snippets, no pagination

   449	      summaryLayer: generateSummaryLayer(results),
   450	      results: extractSnippets(results),
   451	      continuation: null,
   452	    };
   453	  }
   454	
   455	  // First page of snippets
   456	  const firstPage = results.slice(0, pageSize);
   457	  const snippets = extractSnippets(firstPage);
   458	
   459	  // Summary covers ALL results, not just the first page
   460	  const summaryLayer = generateSummaryLayer(results);
   461	
   462	  // Cursor for the remainder
   463	  const continuation = createCursor(results, pageSize, query, options);

 succeeded in 0ms:
   346	 * Resolve a continuation cursor to the next page of results.
   347	 *
   348	 * @param cursor - Opaque cursor token from createCursor.
   349	 * @param pageSize - Number of results to return. Default: 5.
   350	 * @param options - Optional cursor configuration (TTL).
   351	 * @returns Object with next page of results, next cursor (if more), or null if cursor is invalid/expired.
   352	 * @example
   353	 * ```ts
   354	 * const page = resolveCursor(cursor, 5);
   355	 * ```
   356	 */
   357	function resolveCursor(
   358	  cursor: string,
   359	  pageSize: number = DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE,
   360	  options?: CursorOptions,
   361	): { results: DisclosureResult[]; continuation: CursorInfo | null } | null {
   362	  const ttlMs = options?.ttlMs ?? DEFAULT_CURSOR_TTL_MS;
   363	  const payload = decodeCursor(cursor);
   364	  if (!payload) return null;
   365	  const storeKey = payload.cursorKey ?? payload.queryHash;
   366	
   367	  if (
   368	    typeof payload.scopeKey === 'string'
   369	    && payload.scopeKey.length > 0
   370	    && payload.scopeKey !== options?.scopeKey
   371	  ) {
   372	    return null;
   373	  }
   374	
   375	  pruneExpiredCursorEntries(ttlMs);
   376	
   377	  // Check expiry
   378	  const now = Date.now();
   379	  if (now - payload.timestamp > ttlMs) {
   380	    // Expired cursor — clean up stored data
   381	    cursorStore.delete(storeKey);
   382	    return null;
   383	  }
   384	
   385	  // Retrieve stored result set
   386	  const stored = cursorStore.get(storeKey);
   387	  if (!stored) return null;
   388	
   389	  // Extract the requested page
   390	  const start = payload.offset;
   391	  const end = start + pageSize;
   392	  const pageResults = stored.results.slice(start, end);
   393	
   394	  if (pageResults.length === 0) {
   395	    cursorStore.delete(storeKey);
   396	    return null;
   397	  }
   398	
   399	  // Create a new cursor for the next page if there are more results
   400	  let continuation: CursorInfo | null = null;
   401	  if (end < stored.results.length) {
   402	    const nextPayload: CursorPayload = {
   403	      cursorKey: payload.cursorKey,
   404	      offset: end,
   405	      queryHash: payload.queryHash,
   406	      timestamp: payload.timestamp, // Preserve original timestamp for TTL
   407	    };
   408	    continuation = {
   409	      cursor: encodeCursor(nextPayload),
   410	      remainingCount: stored.results.length - end,
   411	    };
   412	  }
   413	
   414	  return { results: pageResults, continuation };

# Iteration 008: COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL

## Focus
COMPARISON - RETRIEVAL: Compare search/retrieval against our Spec Kit Memory (`memory_search`, CocoIndex, code-graph).

## Findings
### Finding 1: Engram has a deterministic `topic_key` retrieval lane that Public does not expose yet
- **Source**: [external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [external/internal/store/store.go#L3201](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3201), [memory-search.ts#L492](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L492), [memory-search.ts#L772](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L772)
- **What it does**: Engram checks for slash-shaped queries, performs an exact `topic_key` lookup first, assigns those hits a forced priority, then merges them ahead of normal FTS results. `SuggestTopicKey()` generates stable family/key pairs such as `architecture/...` or `decision/...`.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public’s `memory_search` is much stronger overall, but in the inspected handler/pipeline surface it still starts from query/concepts/cursor and hybrid routing, not an explicit stable thread key. For recurring decisions and evolving investigations, a deterministic exact-key lane would reduce dependence on fuzzy recall.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Engram’s progressive disclosure is better at “show me the surrounding session” than Public’s current pagination
- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L415](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L415), [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L441](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L441), [external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L74](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L74), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L346](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L346), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L432](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L432), [memory-search.ts#L1023](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L1023)
- **What it does**: Engram’s retrieval path is `mem_search` preview -> `mem_timeline` around one observation -> `mem_get_observation` full body. Public already has summary/snippet/cursor progressive disclosure, but its follow-up step is pagination, not chronology around a chosen hit.
- **Why it matters for us**: Public is more token-efficient at scale, but it still lacks a dedicated “local timeline around result X” retrieval affordance. That would pair well with session continuity and make hit-followup more precise than generic continuation pages.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 3: Engram’s core search is a useful lexical safety baseline, but it is not strong enough to replace Public’s hybrid retrieval stack
- **Source**: [external/internal/store/store.go#L1518](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1518), [external/internal/store/store.go#L3382](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L3382), [pipeline/README.md#L34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md#L34), [hybrid-search.ts#L450](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L450), [hybrid-search.ts#L972](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L972)
- **What it does**: Engram sanitizes user input into quoted FTS5 terms and orders results by `fts.rank`, with the `topic_key` shortcut as the only strong override. Public’s `memory_search` runs a staged pipeline with hybrid candidate generation, signal fusion, reranking, evidence-gap annotation, and weighted FTS/BM25 alongside vector and graph channels.
- **Why it matters for us**: Engram’s search is easier to reason about, but it would be a downgrade for Public’s memory retrieval quality. The right import is its deterministic key shortcut and input-hardening mindset, not its ranking model.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Public’s retrieval architecture is intentionally split across memory, semantic code search, and structural graph search; Engram should not collapse that separation
- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124), [context-server.ts#L755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [context-server.ts#L790](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L790), [memory-context.ts#L1452](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1452), [code-graph/query.ts#L326](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/code-graph/query.ts#L326)
- **What it does**: Engram presents one memory-oriented MCP surface. Public explicitly routes semantic code questions toward CocoIndex, structural dependency/caller questions toward `code_graph_query`, and session/memory recall toward `memory_search` and `memory_context`.
- **Why it matters for us**: This split is one of Public’s biggest retrieval advantages. Engram’s model is good for persistent memory, but it has no equivalent to our semantic code lane or graph lane, so its single-surface simplicity should not become a design target for code retrieval.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval safety boundaries are materially stronger than Engram’s project-scoped filtering
- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625), [external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [memory-search.ts#L531](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L531), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L367](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L367), [memory-triggers.ts#L207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L207), [memory-triggers.ts#L291](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L291)
- **What it does**: Engram narrows retrieval with normalized `project` and optional `scope`. Public binds search continuation to a serialized scope key, validates caller-supplied session IDs against server-trusted sessions, and fail-closes trigger matches when scope filtering cannot be enforced.
- **Why it matters for us**: Once retrieval includes cursors, dedup, and multi-agent continuity, string-level project scoping is not enough. Engram’s ergonomics are fine, but its retrieval boundary model is weaker than Public’s tenant/user/agent/shared-space discipline.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Sources Consulted
- [external/AGENTS.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/AGENTS.md)
- [CLAUDE.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/CLAUDE.md)
- [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go)
- [external/internal/store/store.go](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go)
- [external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts)
- [memory-context.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.68
- Questions addressed: Q3, Q5, Q8, Q9, Q10
- Questions answered: Q3, Q5, Q8, Q9, Q10

## Reflection
- What worked: tracing `Search()` and MCP registration first exposed Engram’s real retrieval contract quickly, and reading Public’s `memory_search` pipeline plus routing/graph handlers made the comparison concrete instead of architectural hand-waving.
- What did not work: CocoIndex was not usable in this session, so I fell back to `rg` plus direct file reads; the filesystem is also read-only here, so I did not update the phase folder or save memory with `generate-context.js`.

## Recommended Next Focus
Trace Engram’s export/import and sync replay next, then compare that against Public’s session bootstrap/resume and shared-scope retrieval to see whether stable topic keys survive cross-install merges without weakening Public’s stronger scope and provenance model.

- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L124), [context-server.ts#L755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [context-server.ts#L790](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L790), [memory-context.ts#L1452](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-context.ts#L1452), [code-graph/query.ts#L326](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/code-graph/query.ts#L326)
- **What it does**: Engram presents one memory-oriented MCP surface. Public explicitly routes semantic code questions toward CocoIndex, structural dependency/caller questions toward `code_graph_query`, and session/memory recall toward `memory_search` and `memory_context`.
- **Why it matters for us**: This split is one of Public’s biggest retrieval advantages. Engram’s model is good for persistent memory, but it has no equivalent to our semantic code lane or graph lane, so its single-surface simplicity should not become a design target for code retrieval.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public’s retrieval safety boundaries are materially stronger than Engram’s project-scoped filtering
- **Source**: [external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go#L625), [external/internal/store/store.go#L1462](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/store/store.go#L1462), [memory-search.ts#L531](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L531), [progressive-disclosure.ts#L367](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts#L367), [memory-triggers.ts#L207](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L207), [memory-triggers.ts#L291](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-triggers.ts#L291)
- **What it does**: Engram narrows retrieval with normalized `project` and optional `scope`. Public binds search continuation to a serialized scope key, validates caller-supplied session IDs against server-trusted sessions, and fail-closes trigger matches when scope filtering cannot be enforced.
- **Why it matters for us**: Once retrieval includes cursors, dedup, and multi-agent continuity, string-level project scoping is not enough. Engram’s ergonomics are fine, but its retrieval boundary model is weaker than Public’s tenant/user/agent/shared-space discipline.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.68
- Questions addressed: Q3, Q5, Q8, Q9, Q10
- Questions answered: Q3, Q5, Q8, Q9, Q10

## Reflection
- What worked: tracing `Search()` and MCP registration first exposed Engram’s real retrieval contract quickly, and reading Public’s `memory_search` pipeline plus routing/graph handlers made the comparison concrete instead of architectural hand-waving.
- What did not work: CocoIndex was not usable in this session, so I fell back to `rg` plus direct file reads; the filesystem is also read-only here, so I did not update the phase folder or save memory with `generate-context.js`.

## Recommended Next Focus
Trace Engram’s export/import and sync replay next, then compare that against Public’s session bootstrap/resume and shared-scope retrieval to see whether stable topic keys survive cross-install merges without weakening Public’s stronger scope and provenance model.
